01KWM0MPS5EBY2H92DR3YN4CVC.description: This GitHub Actions workflow defines the Continuous Integration (CI) process for the Nectar project. It includes jobs for typechecking, testing across multiple operating systems, and a nightly live Deep Lake round-trip canary.

01KWM0MPZ4X7YFJAWBA88VVGJ2.title: Release Workflow for Nectar (npm, OIDC, Trusted Publishing)
01KWM0MPZ4X7YFJAWBA88VVGJ2.description: This GitHub Actions workflow automates the release process for the `@legioncodeinc/nectar` package to npm. It implements OIDC Trusted Publishing for secure, tokenless deployments and includes comprehensive checks, build steps, and a separate publish job with minimal privileges.

01KWM0MQ56J34WCSKMFBNM7ZWB.title: Git Ignore File
01KWM0MQ56J34WCSKMFBNM7ZWB.description: This file specifies intentionally untracked files that Git should ignore. It includes common patterns for logs, dependency directories, build outputs, and development environment files for various programming languages and tools.

01KWM0MQAYTNS4EJD3X6TP92GA.title: Nectar Repository Guidance for ZCode Agents
01KWM0MQAYTNS4EJD3X6TP92GA.description: This document provides comprehensive guidance for ZCode agents working on the Nectar repository. It details what Nectar is (a functional TypeScript/Node semantic memory layer), its current implementation status, repository layout, and crucial design decisions and conventions to follow.

01KWM0MQGS1MCNNHPR1AS504C0.title: GNU Affero General Public License v3
01KWM0MQGS1MCNNHPR1AS504C0.description: This document outlines the GNU Affero General Public License, Version 3. It details the terms and conditions for copying, distributing, and modifying software, with a specific focus on network server software to ensure community access to modified source code.

01KWM0MQPKHKJH9D5P2PGBXA2N.title: Nectar: Stable File Identity and Semantic Memory for AI Agents
01KWM0MQPKHKJH9D5P2PGBXA2N.description: This README introduces Nectar, a system that provides stable identities (nectars) and LLM-minted descriptions for files in a repository, allowing AI agents to recall file meaning even after refactoring. It details how Nectar tracks file identity through renames, moves, and edits, and integrates with Deeplake for durable storage and hybrid recall. The document covers installation, CLI usage, core features, and its underlying architecture.

01KWM0MQWAKXHKQYGZSVMSQVMY.description: This is the domain-knowledge Bee for the Hivenectar design corpus, a semantic memory layer specification. It's responsible for authoring, extending, auditing, and translating documents within the Hivenectar corpus, housed at `library/knowledge/`. This Bee ensures corpus integrity by grounding all factual claims in cited source documents and preserving deliberate specification gaps.

01KWM0MR21031RJJ2V7P04189E.title: ActiveLoop Full Mark Logo (SVG, Dark Background)
01KWM0MR21031RJJ2V7P04189E.description: This SVG file contains the ActiveLoop full mark logo, designed for use on dark backgrounds. 01KWM0MR7Y9A7Q2HZJBZW95GVZ.title: Activeloop Full Mark Logo SVG
01KWM0MR7Y9A7Q2HZJBZW95GVZ.description: This SVG file contains the full mark logo for Activeloop. It's a vector graphic asset likely used for branding and display purposes.

01KWM0MRDXN1VKYYY2F8BM8TDB.description: An SVG file defining a major divider, typically used for visual separation in UI layouts. It's a simple rectangular shape with rounded corners, filled with a specific hex color.

01KWM0MRSXE2RDET8M2G36BVKW.description: This SVG file contains the Legion logo optimized for display on dark backgrounds. It includes both the symbol and wordmark components of the brand identity.

01KWM0MRZSBH7JRR4YYVNKZEXN.description: SVG image of the Legion logo, consisting of a symbol and a wordmark. Designed for use on light backgrounds.

01KWM0MS5J0A3TKWNX1961BVZM.description: This SVG file defines the primary brand mark for Nectar. It features a stylized drop shape with a gradient fill and an inner hexagonal element, all designed for scalability and web display.

01KWM0MSBC0D51W08EF09MZ2RC.description: This SVG file contains the Nectar wordmark logo, designed for high-contrast presentation. It features a bold black 'nectar' text alongside an icon resembling a flower or droplet, with a golden gradient fill.

01KWM0MSH3KCBAHCTSN02R0YMP.description: An SVG file displaying the 'nectar' wordmark. It is designed for use on dark backgrounds, featuring a gradient-filled 'N' icon and text in a light color.

01KWM0MSQ7H57GVFEQGHTCQJ1H.title: Documentation Library Root README
01KWM0MSQ7H57GVFEQGHTCQJ1H.description: This README serves as the root documentation for the repository's library, adhering to schema v2. It outlines the top-level folder structure for knowledge, requirements, issues, and notes.

01KWM0MSX9FZ6XX047Z6T3RZE1.title: Issues Documentation Folder README
01KWM0MSX9FZ6XX047Z6T3RZE1.description: This README describes the structure and lifecycle of Reactive Bug and Incident Work (IRDs) within the 'issues' folder. It outlines sub-folders for backlog, in-work, and completed stages, and naming conventions for IRD documents.

01KWM0MT3GQGMP2NXM8SVJV401.title: Issues Backlog Documentation README
01KWM0MT3GQGMP2NXM8SVJV401.description: This README describes the 'backlog' sub-folder within 'issues,' where IRDs (Incident Response Documents) for tracked but unstarted fixes are stored. It specifies the naming conventions and creation process for new IRDs.

01KWM0MT9GY71M2YTRW80B5227.title: Completed IRD Issues
01KWM0MT9GY71M2YTRW80B5227.description: This directory contains folders for resolved IRDs (Issue Resolution Documents). Entire `ird-<###>-<slug>/` folders are moved here once their corresponding GitHub issue is closed and verified. Files in this directory are read-only and should not be edited after landing.

01KWM0MTFHWZB2YNK5GZ0ZRZDK.title: Issues In-Work Documentation README
01KWM0MTFHWZB2YNK5GZ0ZRZDK.description: This README details the 'in-work' sub-folder for IRDs (Incident Response Documents) that are actively being resolved. It explains the movement of IRD folders through different lifecycle stages.

01KWM0MTNWWRD3C7V270R5DCNN.title: Reference Documentation Structure
01KWM0MTNWWRD3C7V270R5DCNN.description: This document outlines the organization of reference documentation within the repository, separating content by intended audience into 'public/' for end-users and 'private/' for internal team and AI agents. It provides guidelines for where to file new documents and explains the decision process for public versus private content.

01KWM0MTW52X5FJN2K3WJFA44W.title: Internal Documentation Guidelines (Private)
01KWM0MTW52X5FJN2K3WJFA44W.description: This document details the structure for internal engineering and business documentation. It specifies locations for Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) and standards, and lists optional domain folders for various topics. It also clarifies what content does not belong in this private section and outlines the mapping of domain folders to core and deep-dive documents.

01KWM0MV2FZRFJCWAGZYMDQE09.title: Brooding Pipeline: Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM0MV2FZRFJCWAGZYMDQE09.description: This document summarizes the outcome of the 'brooding' process, which is a one-time project scan to mint stable identities and initial descriptions for files. It outlines the specific deliverables, clarifies non-goals, provides a cost predictability summary, and points to related documents for further details on steady-state enrichment, identity minting, projection bootstrapping, and schema.

01KWM0MV8QCBBDQ5VZXAJ7QAD7.title: Brooding Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM0MV8QCBBDQ5VZXAJ7QAD7.description: This document outlines how the 'brooding' process integrates with the broader Nectar AI ecosystem. It traces a file's journey from initial discovery through description and projection, highlighting the interactions with other components like the enricher and content-hash-based inheritance for fresh clones. The document emphasizes how brooding's one-time cost per project is minimized for subsequent users.

01KWM0MVEN01RZXEBG1PPXT33T.title: Brooding Introduction and Theory
01KWM0MVEN01RZXEBG1PPXT33T.description: This conceptual essay explains the 'brooding' mode in an AI system, focusing on why it exists as a distinct process and its economic implications. It describes how aggressive batching with long-context models and a committed projection leads to a one-time, affordable cost for codebase analysis. The document emphasizes the 'why' behind technical decisions.

01KWM0MVMVY3B2XP9QVMFSVBP1.title: Brooding Pipeline: Technical Specification
01KWM0MVMVY3B2XP9QVMFSVBP1.description: This document specifies the technical contract for the 'brooding' pipeline, detailing the stages from file discovery to projection regeneration. It defines bucketing criteria, LLM call shapes, embedding procedures, resumability, estimated costs, and CLI interface. It serves as an authoritative reference for implementing or auditing the brooder.

01KWM0MVVG0M48D28F0CAK8CPT.title: Brooding User Stories
01KWM0MVVG0M48D28F0CAK8CPT.description: This document outlines user stories for the brooding pipeline, covering behaviors the brooder must implement and operator workflows. It includes acceptance criteria for each story, focusing on engineering scope rather than product specifications.

01KWM0MW1RC0PP9CYZMHBTQ5Z0.title: Brooding Pipeline for Semantic Code Memory
01KWM0MW1RC0PP9CYZMHBTQ5Z0.description: This document outlines the 'brooding pipeline,' the initial full-codebase scan that generates semantic descriptions for source files. It details how files are discovered, categorized, and processed using LLMs, covering cost scaling and resumability. This process is crucial for bootstrapping a semantic code memory system.

01KWM0MW7ZQZJ2J40MEE7T8F49.title: Enricher and LLM Model for Semantic Code Memory
01KWM0MW7ZQZJ2J40MEE7T8F49.description: This document details the Nectar system's enricher, a steady-state mechanism for generating and maintaining semantic descriptions of code files. It explains why Gemini 2.5 Flash is the preferred LLM, the debouncing and rate-limiting strategies employed, and how embeddings and failure modes are handled.

01KWM0MWE0JZQSBNXZBRSEECG1.title: Enricher Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM0MWE0JZQSBNXZBRSEECG1.description: This document summarizes the core deliverable of the enricher deep dive: a lazy, debounced, cost-capped enrichment loop for keeping file descriptions fresh. It details the model choice's defensibility, the graceful degradation contract, and provides forward pointers to related documentation. The enricher ensures semantic memory remains current as codebases evolve.

01KWM0MWM1RXGDQFBN64G9CD9Q.title: Enricher Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM0MWM1RXGDQFBN64G9CD9Q.description: This document details the complete lifecycle of a code change through the enricher system, from file modification to becoming recallable. It describes both a steady-state edit flow and a model-swap scenario, highlighting shared mechanics and distinct triggers. The architecture emphasizes composability and auditable state transitions for clear system behavior.

01KWM0MWT0V12F8AWFB3ZRV354.title: Enricher Introduction and Theory
01KWM0MWT0V12F8AWFB3ZRV354.description: This document introduces the enricher, a system for continuous, steady-state code description maintenance. It explains why the enricher is distinct from initial bootstrap processes, details the rationale for selecting Gemini 2.5 Flash, and emphasizes the crucial role of long context windows. The article also discusses the importance of a configurable, auditable model choice and the concept of 'laziness' in processing changes.

01KWM0MX0TE8EZ68S6BKDFFTS7.title: Enricher Technical Specification
01KWM0MX0TE8EZ68S6BKDFFTS7.description: This document outlines the technical specification for the Nectar Enricher, covering its operational contract, architecture, and failure handling. It details the steady-state description-maintenance loop, including polling, debouncing, meaningful-change heuristics, and model selection. The specification also addresses rate-limiting, cost-capping, quality assurance, and the embeddings layer used for recall.

01KWM0MX7A2G1GAKR3WEMZJ5NN.title: Enricher Loop: User Stories
01KWM0MX7A2G1GAKR3WEMZJ5NN.description: This document presents user stories for the lazy enrichment loop, covering personas such as the enricher loop itself, operators, and reviewers. It outlines expectations for polling, configuration, auditing, change detection, and cost control, with acceptance criteria linked to the enricher's observable behavior.

01KWM0MXDRNPP1S0Q0EES7NQ4B.title: Nectar Identity and Re-association Algorithm
01KWM0MXDRNPP1S0Q0EES7NQ4B.description: This document describes Nectar's core algorithm for managing file identity using ULIDs, including how nectars are minted, survive file changes, and are re-associated after offline modifications. It also explains how copy-paste operations are handled to preserve provenance.

01KWM0MXKQC151692MCRATZ5DD.title: Re-association: Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM0MXKQC151692MCRATZ5DD.description: This document outlines the conclusion and deliverables for the re-association process, defining it as a deterministic-exact-then-fuzzy-conservative ladder that prioritizes history integrity. It establishes a 'hard contract' of four invariants that the re-association implementation must uphold to prevent history corruption. The document also provides forward pointers to related architectural decisions, first-run behaviors, fresh-clone inheritance mechanisms, and the schema for data written by the re-association ladder.

01KWM0MXSZST5JDPQ9PN1PD667.title: Re-association: Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM0MXSZST5JDPQ9PN1PD667.description: This document illustrates the journey of a single file through the Nectar re-association system, from its initial minting to various edits, moves, copies, and cloning. It demonstrates how the re-association ladder interacts with the enricher queue and the projection sync, showing the system's continuity and coupling across different operational modes. The document details step-by-step processes for scenarios like live edits, offline file movements, and copy-pasting by teammates, culminating in how a fresh clone inherits file identities and descriptions.

01KWM0MXZWKQGMXS8808B48CQK.title: Re-association: Introduction and Theory
01KWM0MXZWKQGMXS8808B48CQK.description: This conceptual essay explains the reasoning behind Nectar's re-association ladder, focusing on why cold catch-up is challenging and why the algorithm prioritizes conservatism. It details how copy-paste is treated as a first-class provenance edge and describes the portable projection's role in simplifying re-association.

01KWM0MY68FR2BS4W9HJ6WXY2R.title: Re-association: Technical Specification
01KWM0MY68FR2BS4W9HJ6WXY2R.description: This document specifies the technical details of the re-association ladder, an algorithmic contract for identifying and tracking files in a semantic code memory system. It outlines five steps each with an input state, comparison predicate, Deep Lake write actions, post-conditions, and confidence handling. The document also describes the TypeScript functions for minting, copy detection, and fuzzy matching, along with the semantics of the confidence field, the review surface, and the prune grace period.

01KWM0MYDAH9ER1Q22R40ERQSP.title: Re-association: User Stories
01KWM0MYDAH9ER1Q22R40ERQSP.description: This document outlines user stories for the re-association ladder, focusing on the interactions of the daemon, watcher, reviewers, and operators. It details the expected behavior for each step of the re-association process—including exact matches, content changes, moves, fuzzy matches, and new mints—with acceptance criteria for implementation and verification.

01KWM0MYKNNSS5T103J7P3NV86.title: ADR-0001: Minted ULID Nectar for File Identity
01KWM0MYKNNSS5T103J7P3NV86.description: This Architecture Decision Record (ADR) details the decision to use a daemon-minted ULID as the stable identity for files, over source-embedded serials or content-hash-only approaches. It explains the context, decision drivers, rejected alternatives, and consequences of this choice for Nectar's file identity model.

01KWM0MYT4JRRZ46X7HFAH7VS1.title: ADR-0002: Nectar as an Independent Daemon Supervised by Doctor
01KWM0MYT4JRRZ46X7HFAH7VS1.description: This ADR details the decision to separate Nectar from the Honeycomb daemon, making it an independent OS process supervised by 'doctor'. This change allows for independent release cycles, better failure isolation, and standalone operability, while maintaining shared data infrastructure with Honeycomb.

01KWM0MZ09ZV7CMQFZKYYYGA3S.title: ADR-0003: Three-Daemon Topology and hive Portal
01KWM0MZ09ZV7CMQFZKYYYGA3S.description: This architecture decision record establishes Nectar's process topology, defining roles for doctor as supervisor, hive as an always-on portal, and Honeycomb/Nectar as workload daemons. It outlines the operational boundaries and interfaces between these three core components. The document also details the rationale, consequences, and rejected alternatives for this architectural approach.

01KWM0MZ67SEXFBSS2CRD4ZK80.title: ADR-0004: Hive Portal Daemon Role and Boundaries
01KWM0MZ67SEXFBSS2CRD4ZK80.description: This architecture decision record (ADR) defines the role, boundaries, and 'always-on' contract of the Hive portal daemon, a critical component in the three-daemon topology. It addresses how Hive ensures an always-on dashboard, aggregates data from other daemons, manages dashboard ownership and code reuse, and maintains an independent update cadence. Hive serves a static shell and aggregates data from registered daemons, reusing Honeycomb's dashboard code, and operating on its own release schedule to ensure high availability and flexible development.

01KWM0MZCJBFT3P8G8059GBFK4.title: hive Portal Daemon Design Reference
01KWM0MZCJBFT3P8G8059GBFK4.description: Comprehensive design document for hive, the Nectar ecosystem's unified dashboard daemon. It details component breakdown, API aggregation, dashboard ownership, deployment, and lifecycle. This acts as a narrative companion to various ADRs and PRDs.

01KWM0MZJZSFQ8ZB7PEW72ADY5.title: Identity Model — Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM0MZJZSFQ8ZB7PEW72ADY5.description: This document concludes the identity model narrative, restating the adopted Option C, analyzing its consequences, and detailing its reversibility. It also provides a quick reference rejecting alternative options and lists the key deliverables of this architectural decision. The core idea is that file identity must be assigned and maintained by observation rather than relying on inherent file properties.

01KWM0MZS3SSHJFYB61MW1741H.title: Identity Model — Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM0MZS3SSHJFYB61MW1741H.description: This document outlines the cascading effects of the chosen identity model (daemon-minted ULID, never embedded in source) throughout the Nectar system. It traces the lifecycle of a nectar's identity from minting to recall queries, explaining how each stage is shaped by the foundational identity decision and the rejection of alternative approaches.

01KWM0MZZGS15WY5EZCC09MJZ3.title: Identity Model – Introduction and Theory
01KWM0MZZGS15WY5EZCC09MJZ3.description: This document introduces the core theory behind Nectar's identity model, emphasizing why file identity isn't inherent to the file itself but assigned by the system. It explains the concept of 'nectars' (ULIDs) as stable identifiers and why this specific model was chosen as the least-reversible design decision, detailing the failures of alternative identity models.

01KWM0N05DQQ97ZKM8B2CANCKA.title: Identity Model — Technical Specification
01KWM0N05DQQ97ZKM8B2CANCKA.description: This document specifies the technical contract for Nectar's chosen identity model (Option C: daemon-minted ULID). It outlines the non-negotiable invariants, including the nectar format, minting triggers, primary-key contract, universal applicability, and the no-source-mutation rule. It also details how this model satisfies key decision drivers where alternative options fail.

01KWM102HG7TW6PPY6GS60N53X.title: DeepLake Transport Transient-Retry Contract Test
01KWM102HG7TW6PPY6GS60N53X.description: Tests the `HttpDeepLakeTransport`'s retry mechanism for transient errors (e.g., '500: failed to get database connection' or 429) to ensure system resilience and prevent broods from aborting. It verifies that transient errors are retried up to a bounded limit, while non-transient errors fail fast.

01KWM1962EQBB2M98CM7DBCSC0.title: Identity Model — Engineering User Stories
01KWM1962EQBB2M98CM7DBCSC0.description: This markdown document outlines engineering and operator user stories for Nectar's identity model, deriving directly from architectural decision drivers. It defines verifiable acceptance criteria for the implementation and deployment of the identity model, focusing on aspects like stability, provenance, and data storage. The document clarifies that it is not a product requirements document, but rather a technical specification of what a correct implementation must guarantee.

01KWM196CBB9PQDDR46M5XKJK3.title: Hive Graph: Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM196CBB9PQDDR46M5XKJK3.description: This document concludes the 'Hive Graph Deep Dive' series, summarizing the deliverables and contracts of the two-table schema (`hive_graph` and `hive_graph_versions`). It outlines the schema's guarantees, emphasizes the two key database queries it optimizes, and describes 'hard contracts' that prevent misuse. The document also illustrates how the schema integrates with other Nectar subsystems like minting, enriching, recall, and projection.

01KWM196M7NZEQATXRRT9XW9HW.title: Hive Graph: Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM196M7NZEQATXRRT9XW9HW.description: This document outlines the "Ecosystem Story Arc" for the Hive Graph, detailing the end-to-end data flow for Nectar tables. It describes the write path from Nectar minting to projection regeneration, the read path for recall queries, and the composite-key invariant differentiating no-op operations from copy signals. Essentially, it's a deep dive into how Nectar's core data structures interact and maintain consistency across various operations without explicit locking.

01KWM196V458NEEPP4G8C2S7KF.title: Hive Graph: Introduction and Theory
01KWM196V458NEEPP4G8C2S7KF.description: This document introduces the core theoretical split behind Nectar's `hive_graph` and `hive_graph_versions` tables. It explains why a single table cannot adequately represent both file identity and its content history, advocating for distinct tables for stability and change. The document draws parallels to Git, Aura, and Mimir to illustrate how a stable identity anchor combined with append-only versions solves critical challenges in tracking file changes across a codebase, and highlights the implications for system design, including the regenerability of projections.

01KWM1971P6AHT76MEX7AMMBRS.title: Hive Graph: Technical Specification
01KWM1971P6AHT76MEX7AMMBRS.description: This document provides a technical specification for the Hive Graph, detailing the `hive_graph` and `hive_graph_versions` Deep Lake tables. It covers their DDL, column-by-column mutability, indexing strategies, tenancy model, schema healing, and projection contract. The specification also outlines current non-goals to clarify the system's scope and design choices for version 1.

01KWM1979JWJGZ2PSM2K65MWHB.title: Hive Graph: User Stories
01KWM1979JWJGZ2PSM2K65MWHB.description: This document outlines user stories for the `hive_graph` and `hive_graph_versions` schemas, focusing on engineering contracts and acceptance criteria. It covers personas such as schema designers, Deep Lake clients, recall queries, heal passes, and operators. The goal is to ensure data integrity, history completeness, and efficient query mechanisms.

01KWM197G9X5B6MWM2K6DPTA1Z.title: hive_graph_schema
01KWM197G9X5B6MWM2K6DPTA1Z.description: This document specifies the schema for the Hive Graph, a core data model in Nectar. It details two Deep Lake tables: `hive_graph` for stable logical identity and `hive_graph_versions` for append-only content, description chains, and semantic metadata. The document explains the rationale behind the two-table design, column purposes, indexing strategy, tenancy model, and the lazy schema healing contract, providing a foundational understanding of how Nectar tracks and describes code artifacts.

01KWM197PNG49G5RHV02HN6CMQ.title: Portable Registry: Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM197PNG49G5RHV02HN6CMQ.description: This document concludes the portable registry deep-dive, summarizing its core deliverable as a committed, reviewable, and regenerable lockfile for offline identity inheritance in fresh Git clones. It outlines a hard contract of four rules that define what the projection will *not* do, distinguishing it from a sidecar by preventing bloat and enforcing unidirectional synchronization. The document also discusses the trade-offs of committing the projection to a repository versus gitignoring it, and provides forward pointers to related architectural decisions and pipelines.

01KWM197X14FC777TWAAYM3NDC.title: Portable Registry: Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM197X14FC777TWAAYM3NDC.description: This document describes the lifecycle of the portable registry projection (`.honeycomb/nectars.json`) within the Nectar ecosystem. It details how the projection is generated from Deep Lake, consumed by fresh clones for identity inheritance, and updated by brooding and the enricher. The arc emphasizes the one-directional regeneration and the critical role in enabling instant semantic recall for new team members.

01KWM1983D5NVPP9NKW89KCP02.title: Portable Registry
01KWM1983D5NVPP9NKW89KCP02.description: This document introduces the concept of a 'Portable Registry' (specifically, the `nectars.json` file) within the Nectar semantic search system. It explains how this registry resolves the tension between a cloud-based source of truth (Deep Lake) and the need for offline, immediate functionality on a fresh code clone. The document distinguishes a 'projection' (regenerable, satisfying FR-8) from a 'sidecar' (a drifting second source of truth), arguing that the Portable Registry is a projection. It also posits that a current projection enables zero LLM calls and zero fuzzy matches on a fresh clone.

01KWM1989VFCBMNBFPX9DNJZDR.title: Portable Registry: Technical Specification
01KWM1989VFCBMNBFPX9DNJZDR.description: This document specifies the technical details of the Portable Registry, a single JSON file (`.honeycomb/nectars.json`) used for semantic search. It outlines the file format, its contents (and deliberate omissions), the three generation points, validation-on-load contract, projection-invariant enforcement rules, and the atomic write pattern. The specification aims to provide a canonical reference for the projection's structure and behavior within the codebase, crucial for maintaining consistency and reliability across different Honeycomb instances.

01KWM198G5M1GTN295V04QXF10.title: Portable Registry: User Stories
01KWM198G5M1GTN295V04QXF10.description: This document outlines user stories for the 'Portable Registry' feature, detailing how various personas interact with its projection. It covers scenarios like fresh repository clones, daemon operations, code reviews, and manual projection rebuilds. Each story includes acceptance criteria, providing a behavioral contract for the portable registry's implementation.

01KWM198PZ6VDB05DX1W4HMMNE.title: Portable Registry (nectars.json)
01KWM198PZ6VDB05DX1W4HMMNE.description: This markdown document describes the 'Portable Registry' (nectars.json), a critical component for enabling offline access and immediate semantic search capabilities on a fresh git clone of a Deep Lake project. It details the purpose, file format, generation, and validation of this committed JSON projection of the Deep Lake hive_graph table. The document clarifies how the registry helps new clones quickly gain an identity map without network access or costly LLM invocations, distinguishing it from problematic 'sidecar' approaches.

01KWM198X26DNKM0VQJQBWMWQM.title: Recall Integration — Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM198X26DNKM0VQJQBWMWQM.description: This document concludes a deep-dive on 'Recall Integration,' outlining the definitive deliverable as a fourth guarded recall arm. This arm fuses structural answers from CodeGraph with semantic answers from Nectar into a single ranked list, honoring contracts of complementarity and graceful degradation. It also provides essential forward pointers to other documentation for a comprehensive understanding of the design and implementation.

01KWM1993HH23ZVYNMTF83P0W6.title: Recall Integration — Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM1993HH23ZVYNMTF83P0W6.description: This document, titled 'Recall Integration — Ecosystem Story Arc', illustrates the end-to-end flow of a single query through a four-arm guarded recall system. It details how an agent's query is processed, scoped, scored, and fused to produce a ranked list combining CodeGraph structural hits and Nectar semantic hits. The document specifically highlights the roles of the enricher, embedding provider, and BM25 fallback in maintaining the Nectar arm's functionality and its seamless integration with existing recall arms.

01KWM199A0BW4E05YAJ5R4RD82.title: Recall Integration — Introduction and Theory
01KWM199A0BW4E05YAJ5R4RD82.description: This document introduces the concept of "Recall Integration" within the Honeycomb hybrid recall pipeline, focusing on the addition of a 'Nectar' arm. It explains the current limitations of recall (answering what was discussed, not what implements a topic) and proposes Nectar as a semantic solution to bridge this gap. The document argues for integrating Nectar as a fourth guarded arm rather than a separate query due to the benefits of reciprocal rank fusion (RRF) for consolidating diverse information sources.

01KWM199GD50ET6W43473AR49V.title: Recall Integration — Technical Specification
01KWM199GD50ET6W43473AR49V.description: This document specifies the technical details of the Nectar recall arm, which is an integral part of the overall recall system. It defines the SQL contract for retrieving file descriptions, ensuring tenancy isolation, handling versioning, and integrating with existing recall mechanisms. The document details the lexical (BM25) and vector scoring approaches, and how they contribute to the reciprocal rank fusion process. It also outlines guarantees and deliberate non-guarantees of the recall contract.

01KWM199PP6VVDEYPQA7XZJ9NM.description: This document outlines engineering user stories and acceptance criteria for the Nectar recall arm, a component within a larger semantic search index. It describes the behavior, contracts, and failure modes of this arm from the perspective of various personas (agents, recall pipeline, enrichers, operators, engineers). The stories are crucial for defining 'done' for each aspect of the arm and serve as operational ground truth for implementing and verifying its functionality.

01KWM199X0Y90ZXS3YHNG8BZS2.title: Recall Integration
01KWM199X0Y90ZXS3YHNG8BZS2.description: This document outlines how Nectar's `hive_graph_versions` table integrates into the existing Honeycomb hybrid recall pipeline. It explains the mechanics of adding Nectar as a fourth guarded arm, details the weighting and deduplication strategy, and highlights the complementarity between structural CodeGraph queries and Nectar's semantic recall. The document also addresses how Nectar supports team collaboration through cloud synchronization.

01KWM19A3GWNQDXA69N2XF2205.title: Nectar Overview
01KWM19A3GWNQDXA69N2XF2205.description: This markdown document provides a high-level overview of "Nectar," a semantic memory layer designed to give source code files stable identities and human/machine-readable descriptions. It explains Nectar's purpose, how it differs from traditional code indexing, and its architectural components like the `hiveantennae` daemon and its interaction with Deep Lake. The document also outlines Nectar's core design principles and how it integrates with an existing hybrid recall pipeline for semantic search.

01KWM19A9Q4ZHVJJ7KY5DM1182.title: Conclusion and Deliverables of Nectar
01KWM19A9Q4ZHVJJ7KY5DM1182.description: This document, 'Conclusion and Deliverables of Nectar,' summarizes the core outcomes of the Nectar system, outlines its explicit non-goals to define its boundaries, and details measurable success criteria. It clarifies what Nectar delivers, such as stable identity and semantic recall, and what it consciously avoids, like replacing the CodeGraph or mutating source files. Additionally, it features a 'honest novelty claim' and an expanded reading guide to navigate the broader Nectar documentation, solidifying its role as a synthesising overview within the project's knowledge base.

01KWM19AG66J0QXHDEFVEFYSEG.title: Ecosystem Story Arc of Nectar
01KWM19AG66J0QXHDEFVEFYSEG.description: This document outlines the "Ecosystem Story Arc" of Nectar within the broader Honeycomb platform. It describes how Nectar bridges the semantic gap left by the existing structural CodeGraph, detailing their integration via guarded recall fusion and how this combined intelligence is shared across teams using committed projections. The document traces this arc through five key beats and illustrates its impact with a worked example of an agent query flowing through the system, demonstrating how both structural and semantic hits are returned to provide a comprehensive understanding.

01KWM19APKW7W39XAXNG3057B5.title: Nectar Introduction and Theory
01KWM19APKW7W39XAXNG3057B5.description: This document introduces Nectar, a system designed to provide semantic identity for code, complementing the existing structural CodeGraph. It explains the core problem Nectar solves (the gap between structural and semantic understanding), the meaning behind its name, and its three design pillars: stable identity via a daemon-minted nectar, lazy LLM description using a long-context model, and durable state in Deep Lake with a portable projection. The central thesis is that Nectar is a complementary addition, not a replacement, for the CodeGraph, providing probabilistic semantic understanding where the CodeGraph offers deterministic structural insight.

01KWM19AX89GB1DPJQM0JS90YD.description: This document outlines the technical specification for Nectar, focusing on the `hiveantennae` daemon's operating modes, data model contracts, and its role within the larger system. It details how `hiveantennae` processes file changes, manages data in Deep Lake, and integrates with the overall recall pipeline. The document also clearly defines non-goals to set boundaries for its functionality and scope.

01KWM19B3YF1FPHEMN402MP1FM.title: User Stories: Operator and Engineering Scope
01KWM19B3YF1FPHEMN402MP1FM.description: This document outlines detailed user stories for the Nectar system, focusing on the operator and engineering perspectives. It defines the functional scope for the daemon worker's identity model, description pipeline, two-layer recall, fresh-clone inheritance, and overall daemon integration. The user stories serve as a verification checklist for engineers and operators, specifying acceptance criteria for each requirement to ensure a clear understanding of the system's design and behavior.

01KWM19BA6BY7GSQH56C81D5ZW.title: Prior Art Crosswalk
01KWM19BA6BY7GSQH56C81D5ZW.description: This document, 'Prior Art Crosswalk', surveys existing systems related to Nectar's core functionalities: persistent file identity, LLM-minted code description, and semantic codebase search. It meticulously details where Nectar's design aligns with, diverges from, and genuinely innovates beyond these prior arts. The document aims to acknowledge intellectual predecessors and clearly articulate Nectar's unique contributions, particularly in its specific composition of features and integration with Nectar's Deep Lake substrate.

01KWM19BGMXK05G4WM3T2CXV4F.title: Prior Art — Conclusion and Deliverables
01KWM19BGMXK05G4WM3T2CXV4F.description: This document serves as the concluding piece of Nectar's prior art deep dive, summarizing its unique propositions and differentiators. It reiterates Nectar's narrow defensibility claim, performs a gap analysis against the closest prior art system (Smith), and provides a comprehensive table outlining how Nectar's approach differs from prior-art consensus across various dimensions. The file also guides readers to further architectural decision records and system overviews for implementation details.

01KWM19BQBNM6MVGHGDBG9CPQD.title: Prior Art — Ecosystem Story Arc
01KWM19BQBNM6MVGHGDBG9CPQD.description: This document outlines how Nectar's five core design pillars were shaped by existing 'prior art' in the field. It traces each Nectar design choice to its predecessors, highlighting what insights were borrowed and where Nectar deliberately diverged due to specific composition constraints. The primary purpose is to justify Nectar's unique architectural decisions based on its requirements rather than arbitrary preferences.

01KWM19BXWTJB7YJRHW1DKWV0Z.title: Prior Art — Introduction and Theory of Honest Novelty
01KWM19BXWTJB7YJRHW1DKWV0Z.description: This document is a conceptual essay detailing Nectar's approach to novelty and prior art. It explains why Nectar focuses on claiming originality in the composition and integration of existing capabilities rather than inventing new ones. The essay aims to guide contributors and evaluators on Nectar's philosophy, tracing its intellectual lineages and outlining its deliberate divergences from prior systems to avoid reinventing wheels and overclaiming novelty. It emphasizes the importance of understanding the specific composition that Nectar defends as its unique contribution.

01KWM19C4AFK6VY0HGWZ4KMHD7.title: Prior Art — Technical Specification
01KWM19C4AFK6VY0HGWZ4KMHD7.description: This document provides a detailed technical comparison of Nectar with twelve prior art systems across five key pillars: stable identity, LLM description, semantic store, watcher-driven maintenance, and daemon-minted identity. It includes matrices for borrows, divergences, and a checklist emphasizing Nectar's unique composition. The document serves as a verbatim reference for grounding prior-art claims.

01KWM19CB2MZX1ACXD1BX92Z1H.title: Prior Art — Engineering and Operator User Stories
01KWM19CB2MZX1ACXD1BX92Z1H.description: This document outlines user stories for engineering and operations personnel interacting with a 'prior-art' survey within the Nectar project. It defines personas and their tasks related to justifying novelty, auditing credit claims, and evaluating features against existing solutions. The stories ensure that Nectar's development is well-informed by prior work, avoids duplication, and makes defensible claims about its originality. This is an engineering-operations reference, focusing on internal project processes rather than end-user features.

01KWM19CHWJK2JVZCEFDNZ154N.title: Documentation Framework
01KWM19CHWJK2JVZCEFDNZ154N.description: This document defines the comprehensive documentation framework for the repository, outlining standards for various document types from feature PRDs to API references. It specifies naming conventions, folder structures, universal headers, and writing rules to ensure consistency and maintainability across all project documentation. The goal is to provide a single source of truth for how documentation is created and managed within the codebase.

01KWM19CRCYGFEVD9BG5PDDQHQ.title: Public Knowledge Base README
01KWM19CRCYGFEVD9BG5PDDQHQ.description: This file is the README for the public documentation section of the knowledge base. It outlines the purpose, structure, and content guidelines for customer-facing documentation, emphasizing that all content within this directory may eventually be published externally.

01KWM19CYYNFK0JBRY7S3PRXY3.title: Nectar Basics FAQ
01KWM19CYYNFK0JBRY7S3PRXY3.description: This document provides a Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) guide for Nectar Basics. It addresses fundamental questions about what Nectar is, how it functions without altering existing workflows or source code, its compatibility with AI assistants, and its cost. The FAQ aims to clarify Nectar's core purpose as a semantic memory layer for projects.

01KWM19D5HN6XP9W2QWAABRJ16.title: Nectar Comparison FAQ
01KWM19D5HN6XP9W2QWAABRJ16.description: This file provides a Nectar Comparison FAQ, answering common questions about how Nectar differentiates itself from existing tools like regular code search, AI codebase indexing tools, and AI assistants. It explains Nectar's unique value proposition in providing durable, shareable, file-level semantic understanding of a codebase. The document aims to clarify Nectar's role and how it complements, rather than replaces, other development tools.

01KWM19DBS57WAFPJDKYM4JWMG.title: Nectar Privacy and Cost FAQ
01KWM19DBS57WAFPJDKYM4JWMG.description: This document is a Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) guide for Nectar, focusing on privacy and cost concerns. It addresses questions about how Nectar handles user code (local processing vs. cloud transfer), the economics of its initial scan and subsequent usage across teams, and its offline capabilities. The FAQ aims to build trust by explaining the underlying mechanisms for data handling and cost predictability.

01KWM19DJ8EWBREHXGPN8G60XQ.title: Getting Started With Nectar
01KWM19DJ8EWBREHXGPN8G60XQ.description: This guide provides a comprehensive walkthrough for a new user to Nectar, detailing the initial 'first scan' process for a project. It explains what Nectar does on its first run, how to preview and estimate costs, and how to verify that the scan was successful. Essentially, it serves as a 'getting started' manual for Nectar users, ensuring they can effectively index their codebase for semantic search.

01KWM19DRZQ9QP1KZ4SEXWHKTZ.title: Keeping Descriptions Accurate
01KWM19DRZQ9QP1KZ4SEXWHKTZ.description: This guide explains how Nectar automatically maintains accurate file descriptions as code evolves through edits, renames, moves, and copy-paste operations. It details Nectar's mechanisms for handling changes efficiently, ensuring descriptions remain current without constant re-description or manual intervention. The document emphasizes Nectar's low-cost approach and its ability to preserve understanding across refactoring.

01KWM19DZMSSVS07774G2FK79F.title: Sharing Understanding With Your Team
01KWM19DZMSSVS07774G2FK79F.description: This guide explains how Nectar facilitates sharing codebase understanding across development teams by committing a shared map (`.honeycomb/nectars.json`) to version control. It outlines the process for fresh clones to inherit descriptions instantly and for free, ensuring consistent semantic understanding. The document also addresses how Nectar handles differing descriptions, branch switches, and stale maps to maintain a unified view of the codebase.

01KWM19E602VWGRSSE8YQ7B3HS.title: How Nectar Helps Your Agent (Overview)
01KWM19E602VWGRSSE8YQ7B3HS.description: This document explains how Nectar enhances an AI coding assistant's ability to understand a codebase. It compares the agent's performance with and without Nectar, focusing on how Nectar enables searching by file purpose rather than just file names.

01KWM19ECH780ARCHRP411BFGF.title: Nectar Glossary
01KWM19ECH780ARCHRP411BFGF.description: This glossary defines key terms used in the Nectar ecosystem, explaining what each term is and why it matters to the user. It covers concepts like memory layer, Nectar (file identity record), and semantic search.

01KWM19EMWNGAECXS73V5EMH0C.title: What is Nectar?
01KWM19EMWNGAECXS73V5EMH0C.description: This markdown document provides a high-level overview of Nectar, a memory layer designed for AI coding assistants. It explains what Nectar is, the problem it solves (improving code search based on meaning rather than just keywords), and how it works by creating plain-language descriptions for code files. The document also clarifies what Nectar is not, distinguishes it from other tools, and highlights the practical benefits for users, such as more relevant file suggestions and fewer dead ends.

01KWM19EVNCGYR0ZA4SRTD3WJ5.title: Execution Ledger
01KWM19EVNCGYR0ZA4SRTD3WJ5.description: This markdown document serves as an execution ledger for several 'the-smoker' runs, detailing the progress and status of various Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) within the codebase. It tracks the completion of Acceptance Criteria (ACs) for each PRD, including implementation details, verification steps, and remediation of issues found during quality and security reviews. The ledger provides a chronological log of work, explicitly naming models and 'worker-bees' involved, and managing dependencies between different waves of development.

01KWM19F2AARN35PAHVJ5P10MG.title: PRD-018 Execution Ledger
01KWM19F2AARN35PAHVJ5P10MG.description: This markdown document serves as an execution ledger for the pre-release close-out of PRD-018, detailing confirmed decisions and the status of various Acceptance Criteria (ACs). It tracks the progress of development and verification activities performed by different 'worker-bees' (development agents). The document outlines a wave plan for implementation, assigns specific models to different development 'bees,' and includes a detailed run log documenting the project's progress and issues. It functions as a single source of truth for the PRD-018 completion run.

01KWM19F93VVJDDBYNV4A3HRSB.title: Brooding Pipeline Review — 2026-07-02
01KWM19F93VVJDDBYNV4A3HRSB.description: This document provides a detailed technical audit of the Nectar brooding pipeline, identifying critical, high, medium, and low-severity issues. It focuses on problems related to persistence, concurrency, memory usage, and batch processing, with evidence and fix directions for each finding.

01KWM19FFS299PDDQYNY3N4B2M.title: Change-Detection / Update-on-Change Review — 2026-07-02
01KWM19FFS299PDDQYNY3N4B2M.description: This review summarizes the current state of Nectar's change-detection pipeline, identifying critical bugs and design gaps. It highlights that the update-on-change mechanism is largely uninstantiated in production, and details issues like lack of cold catch-up, ignore contract drift, and directory rename handling. The document also provides specific evidence and fix directions for each finding.

01KWM19FP9WMMTGDARE8B933YX.title: Daemon lifecycle, lock, API, service & projection review — 2026-07-02
01KWM19FP9WMMTGDARE8B933YX.description: This review analyzes the Nectar daemon's core plumbing, focusing on its lifecycle, locking mechanisms, API, and projection handling. It uncovers a critical bug in the single-instance lock and details several high-severity issues related to stale lock reclamation, PID reuse, graceful shutdown, and daemon deployment with systemd. The document also assesses adherence to architectural decision records (ADRs) and the portable registry specification.

01KWM19FXQDNZARM3PJ4KQQBJW.title: Nectar Pre-Release Review — Executive Summary
01KWM19FXQDNZARM3PJ4KQQBJW.description: This markdown file serves as an executive summary for a pre-release review of the Nectar project. It assesses the project's ability to analyze a codebase, update on change, and recall information. The summary highlights critical findings, identifies issues in different mission legs, and proposes a prioritized order of attack for remediation.

01KWM19G4EG01XQRZSJ0H11T27.title: Recall-Leg Review
01KWM19G4EG01XQRZSJ0H11T27.description: This markdown file is a detailed recall review document outlining findings related to the recall leg of a system, specifically focusing on hive-graph search, embeddings, and the enricher components. It identifies high, medium, and low severity issues, providing context for their location in the codebase and suggesting potential fixes. The document also includes notes on spec-conformance where the implementation deviates from the defined specifications but is not considered a defect.

01KWM19GB22P5VB8ARNPGYAAPE.title: Spec-Drift Review — Nectar Pre-Release Audit
01KWM19GB22P5VB8ARNPGYAAPE.description: This markdown document conducts a "Spec-Drift Review" for the Nectar project, comparing the implementation against its design specifications and public documentation. It identifies numerous discrepancies, categorized by severity, in areas like core functionality wiring, command-line interface, documentation accuracy, and recall mechanisms. The review provides detailed evidence for each drift and concludes with a prioritized list of recommended pre-release actions to align the codebase with its intended behavior and published promises.

01KWM1G9VNMVJS77B5GV81MFMX.title: Notes README
01KWM1G9VNMVJS77B5GV81MFMX.description: This file describes `library/notes` as a human-only scratch space for unstructured thoughts and temporary content. It explicitly states that AI agents must not read from, write to, or reference files within this directory. Content intended for persistent reference should be moved to `knowledge/private/<domain>/`.

01KWM1G9VRCQP3C6TF4CHQF8TC.title: QA Report: Wave C/D/E Smoker Close-out
01KWM1G9VRCQP3C6TF4CHQF8TC.description: This is a quality assurance report for the Wave C/D/E smoker close-out, detailing the audit of several PRD implementations across multiple repositories. It summarizes the verdict, scorecard, critical issues, warnings, suggestions, and AC coverage, concluding with a 'PASS clean' rating.

01KWM1G9VSXYGJYTFC1G8KDCPB.title: Security Audit - Nectar Wave C/D/E Smoker Close-out
01KWM1G9VSXYGJYTFC1G8KDCPB.description: This security audit report covers the Nectar Wave C/D/E smoker close-out across three repositories (nectar, honeycomb, hive). It details the scope, baseline verification, and findings, concluding with no critical, high, or medium issues and documenting three low/defense-in-depth observations.

01KWM1G9VVP56KNQVKKS7M0ZTJ.title: Security Audit - PRD-018 Pre-Release Close-out
01KWM1G9VVP56KNQVKKS7M0ZTJ.description: This security audit report for PRD-018's pre-release close-out identifies and addresses a high-severity prompt-injection vulnerability in the brooding pipeline. It also re-evaluates and documents several low-severity findings carried forward from previous audits, ensuring the fix maintains a minimal blast radius.

01KWM1G9VX81VZ8HGCP0QDA34C.title: README for Backlog Requirements
01KWM1G9VX81VZ8HGCP0QDA34C.description: This document outlines the process for creating and managing Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) in the backlog. It specifies naming conventions, folder structure, and the lifecycle for moving PRDs from backlog to in-work status.

01KWM1G9VZFRA40T3EQTGGNGWA.title: PRD-001: Three-Daemon Topology
01KWM1G9VZFRA40T3EQTGGNGWA.description: This Product Requirements Document (PRD) defines the foundational three-daemon topology for the Nectar ecosystem, consisting of doctor, hive, nectar, and honeycomb roles. It establishes role boundaries, process independence, and shared data layer, superseding previous two-daemon framings and triggering a new ADR.

01KWM1G9W1A00FMMZKX7XPH7MX.title: PRD-001a: Three-Daemon Topology - ADR-0003 Trigger and Four-Role Contract
01KWM1G9W1A00FMMZKX7XPH7MX.description: This document defines a three-daemon software topology (Doctor, Hive, Nectar, Honeycomb) and formally triggers ADR-0003 to record this architectural decision. It details the distinct responsibilities and explicit non-integration points for each role, emphasizing process-layer independence while maintaining a shared data layer. It also specifies the minimal daemon registry for Doctor and the portal daemon role for Hive.

01KWM1G9W244YVE8VWDF5C60RA.title: Nectar Process, Lock, Health, Deep Lake Client, and Tenancy
01KWM1G9W244YVE8VWDF5C60RA.description: This PRD defines the process surface of the Nectar daemon, including its HTTP bind, single-instance PID/lock guard, `/health` endpoint, and Deep Lake client. It details how Nectar operates as a standalone OS process while sharing the same Deep Lake tables and tenancy model as Honeycomb. The document specifies Nectar's graceful shutdown behavior to ensure clean restarts.

01KWM1G9W45V7HZYS515QA3D7F.title: PRD-001c: Shared Infrastructure Consumption
01KWM1G9W45V7HZYS515QA3D7F.description: This document outlines how the Nectar daemon interacts with shared infrastructure components: Portkey, the embeddings daemon, CodeGraph, and the recall engine. It specifies that Nectar uses its own clients for these interactions, emphasizing network-based communication over in-process sharing. A key invariant established is the deploy-time tenancy model ensuring Nectar's data is readable by Honeycomb's recall engine.

01KWM1G9W6J57BJ6RWA7X6BPTS.title: .gitkeep for QA folder
01KWM1G9W6J57BJ6RWA7X6BPTS.description: This is a placeholder file typically used in version control systems. Its sole purpose is to ensure that an empty directory is tracked. It indicates the presence of a 'qa' folder that may contain quality assurance-related files.

01KWM1G9W72S0JMNY977P6PGP3.title: QA Report: PRD-001 Three-Daemon Topology (PRD-vs-Corpus Conformance)
01KWM1G9W72S0JMNY977P6PGP3.description:
This document is a QA report for PRD-001, focusing on its conformance to the Nectar knowledge corpus. It verifies acceptance criteria and descriptions against cited sources, confirming resolution of prior warnings and stating a 'PASS' verdict.
It details resolution of issues like non-resolving markdown links for code references and stale code-path prefixes.

01KWM1G9W88Y330HN5M0QWAKBY.title: PRD-002: Nectar Daemon Index
01KWM1G9W88Y330HN5M0QWAKBY.description: This is the index document for PRD-002, which describes the Nectar daemon, a standalone OS workload responsible for the semantic-memory pipeline. It outlines the daemon's core features including its bootstrap, worker harness, CLI surface, and single-instance lock mechanism. The document emphasizes process-layer independence while maintaining a shared data layer with Honeycomb.

01KWM1G9WAVBG6S3ZE2RQQHH4J.title: PRD-002a: Nectar Bootstrap and Composition Root
01KWM1G9WAVBG6S3ZE2RQQHH4J.description: This document defines the bootstrap sequence and composition root for the Nectar daemon, mirroring Honeycomb's assembly patterns. It outlines the fixed-order construction steps: config load, Deep Lake client, auth/scoping, worker construction, single-instance lock acquisition, lifecycle start, socket bind, and signal handlers. The PRD ensures the daemon boots lazily, without importing Honeycomb's runtime modules.

01KWM1G9WB9NKEYF8WBABBD7MG.title: PRD-002b: Hiveantennae Worker
01KWM1G9WB9NKEYF8WBABBD7MG.description:
This sub-PRD details the hiveantennae worker, the core semantic-memory pipeline loop (watch → re-associate → mint/enrich). It specifies its design as a lease-based harness mirroring honeycomb's stage-worker and its interaction with other PRDs.
The document also outlines the worker's crash-safety, non-blocking guarantees, and covers various operating modes like brooding, live watch, cold catch-up, and projection sync.

01KWM1G9WCRTTJSFYV9PE7HJ2M.title: PRD-002c: Nectar CLI Surface
01KWM1G9WCRTTJSFYV9PE7HJ2M.description: This document describes the command-line interface (CLI) an operator uses to interact with the Nectar daemon. It enumerates commands for daemon lifecycle management, brooding, pruning, reviewing matches, and regenerating projection files. The CLI acts as a thin client, either invoking the daemon's composition root directly or communicating with a running daemon over loopback, mirroring Honeycomb's CLI architecture.

01KWM1G9WEB3FZJAJR1K1YP6MG.title: Single-Instance Lock and Graceful Shutdown
01KWM1G9WEB3FZJAJR1K1YP6MG.description: This PRD defines the single-instance PID/lock guard and graceful shutdown path for the Nectar daemon, mirroring Honeycomb's patterns. It ensures that only one Nectar instance runs at a time, prevents port double-binding, and allows for clean restarts by doctor. The document specifies distinct lock filenames for Nectar and Honeycomb to allow coexistence.

01KWM1G9WHBNA1NVRJX8SZ4MWK.title: QA Report: PRD-002 Nectar Daemon (PRD-vs-Corpus Conformance)
01KWM1G9WHBNA1NVRJX8SZ4MWK.description:
This QA report details the conformance audit of PRD-002, covering the Nectar Daemon and its sub-PRDs, against the Nectar knowledge corpus. It confirms its adherence to design decisions and code patterns, noting its "PASS" status.
The report highlights the resolution of systemic W-1 issues (code reference linking) and provides a scorecard on completeness, correctness, alignment, and gap handling.

01KWM1G9WJ03E0JHMHBS68P0R7.title: Nectar supervision by doctor
01KWM1G9WJ03E0JHMHBS68P0R7.description: This PRD outlines how Nectar becomes a supervised daemon by Doctor, detailing the necessary surfaces and contracts. It specifies the `/health` endpoint, PID/lock file, OS service unit, and registry entry required for Doctor to probe, restart, and maintain Nectar's liveness. The document consumes and conforms to existing process surface definitions.

01KWM1G9WKWY470XVA87WRTVXV.title: /health endpoint + PID/lock file
01KWM1G9WKWY470XVA87WRTVXV.description: This sub-PRD specifies the `/health` endpoint and single-instance PID/lock file for the Nectar daemon, implementing prerequisites for Doctor supervision. It details how Nectar's custom `/health` endpoint will provide coarse status, and how the PID/lock files ensure single-instance operation and enable clean restarts. Both are modeled on existing Honeycomb patterns but are purpose-built for Nectar.

01KWM1G9WNDK1QJ1S2VYE4V41K.title: OS service unit definition + install
01KWM1G9WNDK1QJ1S2VYE4V41K.description: This sub-PRD defines the OS service units (launchd, systemd, Windows Scheduled Task) for the Nectar daemon, enabling start-on-boot and restart-on-crash functionality. It models these units on Doctor's self-registration service module, ensuring Nectar's resilience and manageability. The document details platform-specific configurations and the installation process, prioritizing user-scope installation by default.

01KWM1G9WPC8RYBR9HYKNPG3MA.title: PRD-003c: nectar's registry entry + watchdog-war guards
01KWM1G9WPC8RYBR9HYKNPG3MA.description:
This PRD defines nectar's registry entry within doctor's daemon registry file and the critical watchdog-war guards for its restart rung. It specifies the configuration for nectar to be supervised by doctor, ensuring correct PID file usage and preventing double-binding issues.
The document details the fields of nectar's registry entry, the default values it inherits or overrides, and the mechanism for installer-based registration.

01KWM1G9WQXJZZV8TDK62F5EF3.title: Empty Gitkeep File
01KWM1G9WQXJZZV8TDK62F5EF3.description:
This file is a standard .gitkeep file used to ensure that an otherwise empty directory remains tracked by Git.
It does not contain any functional code or documentation.

01KWM1G9WS73JZQP8CGQ7CA33F.title: QA Report: PRD-003 Nectar Supervision
01KWM1G9WS73JZQP8CGQ7CA33F.description: This document is a QA report for PRD-003 Nectar Supervision, verifying its conformance against the Nectar knowledge corpus. It assesses `/health` and PID/lock contracts, OS service units, and registry entries. The report confirms that PRD-003 passes with medium-and-above standards.

01KWM1G9WTVBJK7EQDHV0PRTPK.title: PRD-004: doctor daemon registry + hive portal daemon
01KWM1G9WTVBJK7EQDHV0PRTPK.description: This is an out-of-band Product Requirements Document (PRD) specifying the generalization of doctor's supervision capabilities to manage multiple daemons via a static registry file, and the introduction of a new hive portal daemon. It defines the foundational three-daemon topology (doctor, hive, nectar/honeycomb). The PRD outlines goals such as isolated supervision instances, state-light doctor, and an always-on hive dashboard, while explicitly stating non-goals like doctor's dashboard logic or runtime registry API.

01KWM1G9WVD0F06K05X89SKTJR.title: PRD-004a: doctor daemon registry — config schema + per-daemon supervisor instanc
01KWM1G9WVD0F06K05X89SKTJR.description: This sub-PRD details the generalization of doctor's single-daemon configuration into a named registry, enabling supervision of multiple daemons. It specifies the registry file schema, the creation of independent supervisor instances per entry, and ensures isolated incident logging and remediation state for each daemon. The document also clarifies that registration is via file edit, not a runtime API, and doctor is still a single OS process with concurrent supervisor loops.

01KWM1G9WXBAGMZGE9C5FB0B0S.title: PRD-004b: doctor status page + CLI — multi-daemon reporting
01KWM1G9WXBAGMZGE9C5FB0B0S.description: This sub-PRD extends doctor's reporting surfaces—its loopback status page and CLI commands—to display information for multiple supervised daemons. It specifies that the status page will show a per-daemon table and the CLI 'status' command will print a block for each daemon. Additionally, the 'logs' command will gain a daemon filter for viewing specific incident streams.

01KWM1G9WYG0959E4YBYGS565T.title: PRD-004c: hive portal daemon
01KWM1G9WYG0959E4YBYGS565T.description: Describes the 'hive' portal daemon, an always-on dashboard serving as a single source of UI truth. It aggregates API data from registered daemons through a server-side proxy.

01KWM1G9X0PVQHDQ7EN0CEBV1Z.title: PRD-004d: hive OS service unit + daemon registration
01KWM1G9X0PVQHDQ7EN0CEBV1Z.description: Details how the 'hive' daemon, and future daemons, become OS-level citizens through service units and registration in doctor's registry. It outlines the process for hive to boot on OS start and be independently updateable.

01KWM1G9X10GVN4GFZ27NA00A0.title: .gitkeep
01KWM1G9X10GVN4GFZ27NA00A0.description: This is a placeholder file used to ensure the 'qa' directory is committed to Git. It contains no meaningful content.

01KWM1G9X28WDBGJWCQRB1ET30.title: QA Report: PRD-004 doctor Registry + hive Portal
01KWM1G9X28WDBGJWCQRB1ET30.description: A conformance audit report for PRD-004, covering the doctor registry and hive portal against the Nectar knowledge corpus. It assesses completeness, correctness, alignment, and identifies any issues or suggestions.

01KWM1G9X4N1P1P96WWTR3E9KW.title: PRD-005: Hive Graph Catalog Tables and Lazy Schema Healing
01KWM1G9X4N1P1P96WWTR3E9KW.description: Defines the data layer for Nectar, registering 'hive_graph' and 'hive_graph_versions' Deep Lake tables as Honeycomb CatalogTable entries. It details their schema, tenancy model, and lazy schema healing mechanism.

01KWM1G9X5GJPXJN3PVT15V29T.title: PRD-005a: `hive_graph` Table — Identity + Provenance
01KWM1G9X5GJPXJN3PVT15V29T.description: `hive_graph` stores stable identity and provenance for logical files, keyed by a daemon-minted ULID. It defines the DDL for this catalog table, registering it with a `tenant` scope and `update-or-insert` write pattern. The table specifically excludes content or descriptions, which are handled in a separate versions table.

01KWM1G9X7M6BPX15MZWMANC3V.title: PRD-005b: `hive_graph_versions` Table — Content + Description Chain
01KWM1G9X7M6BPX15MZWMANC3V.description: `hive_graph_versions` is an append-only table storing content, metadata, and LLM-generated descriptions for file versions. It includes a nullable 768-dimension embedding for semantic recall and uses a composite key to track changes. The table is registered as a `CatalogTable` with `append-only` writes and `tenant` scope.

01KWM1G9X891GNG2H69A459NW1.title: PRD-005c: Tenancy Model + `project_id` Soft-Filter Verification
01KWM1G9X891GNG2H69A459NW1.description: This document specifies the tenancy contract for hive-graph tables, confirming `org_id`, `workspace_id`, and `project_id` columns. It verifies that `project_id` functions as a column-level soft filter within the workspace partition, rather than a partition itself. It also clarifies why `agent_id` and `visibility` columns are absent due to the cross-agent nature of file identity.

01KWM1G9X9KAY6NKCFMTCHCTGY.title: .gitkeep
01KWM1G9X9KAY6NKCFMTCHCTGY.description: This is a placeholder file in the 'qa' directory.

01KWM1G9XBFM8DZ7NKMXDPH7BH.title: QA Report: PRD-005 Hive Graph Catalog Tables
01KWM1G9XBFM8DZ7NKMXDPH7BH.description: This QA report provides a conformance audit of PRD-005 (hive-graph catalog tables) against the Nectar knowledge corpus and Honeycomb code. It identifies zero critical findings, but notes several warnings regarding documentation consistency and broken links. The report details a remediation addendum addressing these warnings.

01KWM1G9XCPN80MKXTMVW6DEY1.title: PRD-006: File Registration Protocol (Create / Move / Delete / Copy-Paste)
01KWM1G9XCPN80MKXTMVW6DEY1.description: This document specifies the file registration protocol, covering create, move, delete, and copy-paste operations within the system. It details how disk changes are observed, debounced, classified, and resolved into Nectar decisions, emphasizing the use of `node:fs.watch` and a 5-step re-association ladder for identity management.

01KWM1G9XEB9S3P2J15DXTXDSV.title: PRD-006a: node:fs.watch Intake + Debounce
01KWM1G9XEB9S3P2J15DXTXDSV.description: This sub-PRD details how the system observes disk changes using `node:fs.watch` and debounces event bursts into settled cycles. It mirrors Honeycomb's `file-watcher.ts` pattern, avoiding `chokidar`, and outlines the workspace scope and ignore contract.

01KWM1G9XFDHR7QW5YCGFZHCPJ.title: PRD-006b: Event -> Ladder-Step Classification
01KWM1G9XFDHR7QW5YCGFZHCPJ.description: This sub-PRD defines how `fs.watch` observations are translated into one of three classes: new path, changed path, or missing path. It specifies the classification algorithm and the importance of the 'missing-files set' for reconstructing moves from uncorrelated events.

01KWM1G9XH3326GKKAPP3JQGVJ.title: PRD-006c: Copy-Event Detection (derived_from_nectar minting)
01KWM1G9XH3326GKKAPP3JQGVJ.description: This sub-PRD describes the copy detector, which identifies new files whose content matches existing files. It outlines how a fresh nectar is minted with provenance through `derived_from_nectar` and `fork_content_hash`, distinguishing copies from moves.

01KWM1G9XJSA9BZ5DD3KPV6SMQ.title: PRD-006d Re-association Ladder
01KWM1G9XJSA9BZ5DD3KPV6SMQ.description: Details the 5-step re-association ladder algorithm for classifying files in the Nectar semantic code memory system. It covers move reconstruction, fuzzy matching, human review processes, and the conservative pruning of nectars. The document emphasizes a 'no-guess' contract for low-confidence matches.

01KWM1G9XMEPYH78CK2V9V99N2.title: .gitkeep
01KWM1G9XMEPYH78CK2V9V99N2.description: This is a placeholder file used to ensure that the 'qa' directory is included in Git. It has no functional content or purpose beyond directory preservation.

01KWM1G9XNV0SGHG54P1E4P16M.title: QA Report: PRD-006 File Registration Protocol
01KWM1G9XNV0SGHG54P1E4P16M.description: This QA report audits PRD-006 (File Registration Protocol) for conformance against the Nectar knowledge corpus and Honeycomb code. It details findings, including critical issues, warnings, and suggestions, related to the specification's accuracy and implementation alignment.

01KWM1G9XQSF5WK5F4MACMPTSX.title: PRD-007 Brooding Process Index
01KWM1G9XQSF5WK5F4MACMPTSX.description: This document outlines the Brooding Process, a one-time full-codebase scan for a Nectar project. It details the pipeline for discovering, describing, and embedding files, including cost estimates, resumability, and CLI interactions. This process initializes the 'hive graph' and portable projection for a project.

01KWM1G9XRE6JGR1MMD46CNK6J.title: PRD-007a Discovery + Content-Hash Pre-Check
01KWM1G9XRE6JGR1MMD46CNK6J.description: This document specifies the discovery and content-hash pre-check stages of the Brooding Process. It details how files are enumerated using CodeGraph's `git ls-files` command and how existing descriptions are inherited via content hash matching to avoid redundant LLM calls. This is crucial for efficient fresh clones.

01KWM1G9XTNG5972GSTTE3G8ZY.title: PRD-007b Bucketing + LLM Call Shapes
01KWM1G9XTNG5972GSTTE3G8ZY.description: This document details how files are categorized into four buckets (skip-binary, skip-too-large, batch, solo) during the Brooding Process, based on size and parsability. It defines the LLM call shapes and prompts for each bucket, as well as the precise cost estimations for processing files. The document emphasizes verbatim adoption of corpus details.

01KWM1G9XV7G2QHSNJD0ZVY819.title: PRD-007c Resumability State Machine
01KWM1G9XV7G2QHSNJD0ZVY819.description: This document specifies the resumability state machine for the Brooding Process, ensuring that a killed or interrupted brood can resume without loss of work or duplicate description costs. It details the three resumption rules based on the `describe_status` column and the deliberate absence of lockfiles. This design mirrors Honeycomb's append-only resumability patterns.

01KWM1G9XWA8QW6Y2J6QXDJWZ6.title: PRD-007d CLI Surface + Dry-Run
01KWM1G9XWA8QW6Y2J6QXDJWZ6.description: This document defines the Command Line Interface (CLI) for controlling the Brooding Process, including the `brood` command and its flags (`--force`, `--limit N`, `--dry-run`). It specifies how brooding is triggered automatically or explicitly, and highlights the `--dry-run` feature for cost estimation without incurring LLM call expenses. The document details the interaction with the resumability state machine.

01KWM1G9XY8QXA7FH9VBCCKRSQ.title: QA Report: PRD-007 Brooding Process
01KWM1G9XY8QXA7FH9VBCCKRSQ.description: This QA report audits PRD-007 (Brooding Process) for conformance against the Nectar knowledge corpus. It identifies a critical issue regarding a batch-size cap contradiction and remediates numerous mechanical defects. The report provides a comprehensive traceability of acceptance criteria and high-risk surfaces.

01KWM1G9XZ2DSGSMM2A5TJ55Z6.title: PRD-008 Nectar Daemon API Endpoints Index
01KWM1G9XZ2DSGSMM2A5TJ55Z6.description: This document defines the HTTP API endpoints exposed by the Nectar daemon for interaction with the dashboard and operators. It covers the `/api/hive-graph` route group, specifying search capabilities, a build trigger, status monitoring, and projection management. The design mirrors Honeycomb's API patterns for scaffolding and permission inheritance.

01KWM1G9Y11ZHFJGB0S1M0XGMH.title: PRD-008a: Route Group Scaffolding
01KWM1G9Y11ZHFJGB0S1M0XGMH.description: This document specifies the scaffolding for the `/api/hive-graph` route group in the Nectar daemon. It defines the `RouteGroup` abstraction, the `mountHiveGraphApi` attach point, and the contract for handler attachment while mirroring Honeycomb’s routing patterns. The primary goal is to ensure all endpoints under this group inherit protection middleware without each handler needing to re-wire authentication.

01KWM1G9Y3TJCN7EZVQJ4BQNSQ.title: PRD-008b: Search Endpoint
01KWM1G9Y3TJCN7EZVQJ4BQNSQ.description: This document defines the `/api/hive-graph/search` HTTP handler, a thin route that exposes PRD-012's search engine for file descriptions. It validates queries, resolves tenant scope, and delegates the search, surfacing failures as data. This endpoint is a standalone search tool and does not fuse into the agent-facing recall.

01KWM1G9Y4M296A36SSV9TN9RR.title: PRD-008c: Build, Status, and Projection Endpoints
01KWM1G9Y4M296A36SSV9TN9RR.description: This document specifies the `/api/hive-graph/build`, `/api/hive-graph/status`, and projection CRUD endpoints. These endpoints allow triggering the brooding pipeline, retrieving pipeline status (queue depth, describe status counts, cost), and managing the `.honeycomb/nectars.json` projection. They share the same scope resolution and error handling patterns as other `/api/hive-graph` endpoints.

01KWM1G9Y6C18V2Z26A4TTZXVZ.title: QA Report: PRD-008 Nectar Daemon API Endpoints
01KWM1G9Y6C18V2Z26A4TTZXVZ.description: This document is a QA report for PRD-008, focusing on the Nectar daemon's API endpoints. It audits the PRD against the knowledge corpus and Honeycomb source code, identifying critical issues and warnings related to the API's implementation assumptions.

01KWM1G9Y7XNNM345C63ZVW2HC.title: PRD-009: Harness exposure via recall extension
01KWM1G9Y7XNNM345C63ZVW2HC.description: This document outlines PRD-009, which records the decision for Nectar to gain agent-facing exposure by extending Honeycomb's shared recall engine, rather than shipping its own harness hooks. It details why a new arm in Honeycomb's recall engine will automatically surface Hive Graph hits in all integrated harnesses.

01KWM1G9Y8632432FG6F3BHD6W.title: PRD-009a: Decision record + per-harness recall-call-site mapping + tenancy-scope
01KWM1G9Y8632432FG6F3BHD6W.description: This sub-PRD for PRD-009 documents the decision against Nectar shipping its own harness hooks, maps how existing harnesses utilize Honeycomb's recall engine, and defines the critical tenancy-scope invariant for successful integration. It verifies that a new recall arm will propagate to all armed harnesses without additional work.

01KWM1G9YAGKWET36PK8RW4S4C.title: .gitkeep
01KWM1G9YAGKWET36PK8RW4S4C.description: This is an empty file used to ensure that the 'qa' directory is included in Git. It has no functional purpose within the system.

01KWM1G9YBQ89SAZ7S1MCMAW9P.title: QA Report: PRD-009 Harness Exposure via Recall Extension
01KWM1G9YBQ89SAZ7S1MCMAW9P.description: This QA report for PRD-009 verifies the documentation-only PRD's claims against the Honeycomb codebase and Nectar's knowledge corpus. It confirms the strategic decision on harness exposure but identifies critical issues in the described recall propagation mechanism.

01KWM1G9YDRECP42A2QXX3NBCX.title: PRD-010: Portkey gateway integration
01KWM1G9YDRECP42A2QXX3NBCX.description: This PRD outlines the integration of Nectar's LLM calls through the existing Portkey gateway. It specifies the reuse of Honeycomb's Portkey transport, the default model (Gemini 2.5 Flash), and how semantic caching and guardrails are handled as server-side Portkey features.

01KWM1G9YF8YY6HF1ADRV87S8Q.title: PRD-010a: Portkey transport reuse
01KWM1G9YF8YY6HF1ADRV87S8Q.description:
This sub-PRD details the reuse of an existing Portkey transport within Nectar for brooding batch and enricher calls.
It outlines how Nectar consumes the shared `ProviderTransport` with existing headers and URL, ensuring secret discipline and usage accounting, without authoring new transport code. The PRD confirms Nectar's integration with the Portkey gateway for LLM calls while reusing existing infrastructure.

01KWM1G9YGV5GMEDXSX0Y96AK1.title: PRD-010b: Model selection and describe_model audit
01KWM1G9YGV5GMEDXSX0Y96AK1.description:
This sub-PRD defines the default LLM for Nectar's file descriptions as Gemini 2.5 Flash, configurable via `activeModel` rather than hardcoded.
It specifies mechanisms for operators to force re-description under a new model (`brood --force --model <new>`) and ensures the `describe_model` column consistently records the producing model for auditing purposes. The document also presents a model comparison table justifying the default model choice.

01KWM1G9YJEWSBE9G4TH0V3H2T.title: PRD-010c: Semantic cache and guardrails (Portkey-server-side)
01KWM1G9YJEWSBE9G4TH0V3H2T.description:
This documentation sub-PRD clarifies that Portkey's semantic caching and guardrails are server-side features, configured via the `portkey.config` ID in the Portkey dashboard, not within Nectar's codebase.
It asserts that Nectar only accounts for upstream cached tokens via the existing `UsageSink` and does not implement client-side caching or guardrail toggles. The PRD explicitly rejects client-side caching to avoid duplication and complexity.

01KWM1G9YKF6V698W6Y6HT3FN3.title: QA Report: PRD-010 Portkey Gateway Integration (PRD-vs-Corpus/Code Conformance)
01KWM1G9YKF6V698W6Y6HT3FN3.description:
This QA report audits PRD-010 and its sub-PRDs (010a/010b/010c) for conformance against the Nectar knowledge corpus and Honeycomb transport code.
It confirms that all acceptance criteria trace to authoritative sources, every cited Honeycomb symbol exists, and key architectural decisions like server-side caching are honestly documented and match the code. The report concludes with a PASS-with-warnings verdict, identifying a minor line-drift citation defect.

01KWM1G9YNFKGZ269WQ063HY8S.title: QA Report: Wave B PRD-010, PRD-011, PRD-014 Implementation Close-Out
01KWM1G9YNFKGZ269WQ063HY8S.description:
This QA report provides a close-out audit for the Wave B implementation of PRD-010, PRD-011, and PRD-014.
It confirms that all 16 module-level acceptance criteria are satisfied at the declared dormant-but-wired bar, with the rebuild CLI wired to `DeepLakeHiveGraphStore`. The report concludes with a PASS verdict, indicating no critical or warning findings, and details the verification results and file changes.

01KWM1G9YQDDNXP9V9WR0RJRBR.title: PRD-011: Portable projection (.honeycomb/nectars.json) sync
01KWM1G9YQDDNXP9V9WR0RJRBR.description:
This PRD defines `.honeycomb/nectars.json` as a committed, regenerable projection of the Deep Lake `hive_graph` table, enabling fresh `git clone` identity maps without network or LLM calls.
It outlines the file format, three generation triggers with atomic writes, validation-on-load, and a fresh-clone inheritance path. The document emphasizes the projection's role as a regenerable lockfile, not a sidecar, and includes its acceptance criteria.

01KWM1G9YRZ9S973JRYNJZ3DE7.title: PRD-011a: Projection format, generation triggers, and atomic write
01KWM1G9YRZ9S973JRYNJZ3DE7.description:
This sub-PRD details the verbatim JSON format for `.honeycomb/nectars.json`, the portable projection of Deep Lake data.
It specifies the three exact generation triggers (brooding completion, enricher activity, explicit rebuild) and mandates an atomic `temp + rename` write pattern to ensure durability and prevent partial file corruption. The document outlines the projection's format fields and deliberate omissions.

01KWM1G9YT1015YXMZXVB8JWXH.title: PRD-011b: Validation on load and fresh-clone inheritance
01KWM1G9YT1015YXMZXVB8JWXH.description:
This sub-PRD defines the daemon's boot process for `.honeycomb/nectars.json`, specifying a four-point validation-on-load contract (version, project triple, ULID, sha256).
It outlines how a valid projection enables zero-LLM fresh-clone inheritance, where matching files on disk inherit their nectar and description. The PRD ensures that invalid projections are ignored, triggering a fallback to full brooding without partial loading.

01KWM1G9YV6PP0G7ACNW4X39S2.title: PRD-011c: rebuild-projection CLI and the projection-not-sidecar invariant
01KWM1G9YV6PP0G7ACNW4X39S2.description:
This sub-PRD covers the `honeycomb nectar rebuild-projection` CLI commands, which facilitate the full regeneration of `.honeycomb/nectars.json` from Deep Lake.
