It establishes three enforcement rules asserting that the projection is a regenerable artifact, not a state-carrying sidecar that could deviate from Deep Lake as the source of truth. The PRD mandates byte-identical output (modulo `generated_at`) to prove this invariant and prevent silent drifts.

01KWM1G9YWXJQZHXJKYH1FE2FG.title: QA Report: PRD-011 Portable Projection Sync (PRD-vs-Corpus Conformance)
01KWM1G9YWXJQZHXJKYH1FE2FG.description:
This QA report audits PRD-011 (index + 011a/011b/011c) for conformance against the Nectar knowledge corpus and cited Honeycomb code, confirming its adherence to documentation frameworks.
It validates the verbatim inclusion of projection format, triggers, validation, and sidecar rules from `data/portable-registry.md`. The report highlights a Warning regarding a sha256 validation discrepancy between the PRD and the in-band hasher, recommending reconciliation before implementation.

01KWM1G9YYQQW8DFZM8V9YRV3N.title: PRD-012: Manual Hive Graph Search
01KWM1G9YYQQW8DFZM8V9YRV3N.description: This PRD describes the manual hive graph search, an operator-facing tool for lexical and semantic search over file descriptions. It details the search engine mechanics, CLI command, and HTTP endpoint for this standalone search capability. The search mirrors the recall engine's patterns but is distinct from the agent-facing fused recall.

01KWM1G9Z0M93GAJ1NEY93TY2Q.title: PRD-012a: Lexical + Semantic Search over the Hive Graph
01KWM1G9Z0M93GAJ1NEY93TY2Q.description: This sub-PRD specifies the `searchHiveGraph` engine, a standalone function performing lexical (BM25) and semantic (vector) search on `hive_graph_versions`. It reuses established patterns from the recall engine for SQL safety, embedding, and fail-soft behavior, focusing on the latest described version of files.

01KWM1G9Z23SB3JCND0H4ZWK1W.title: PRD-012b: CLI and Endpoint Surface
01KWM1G9Z23SB3JCND0H4ZWK1W.description: This sub-PRD defines the two client interfaces for the `searchHiveGraph` engine: the `nectar search <query>` CLI command and the `/api/hive-graph/search` HTTP endpoint. Both surfaces are thin clients, calling the same engine and emitting identical result shapes, with the CLI reaching the daemon over loopback.

01KWM1G9Z4S77DYBSCBH1XN9ZK.title: QA Report: PRD-012 Manual Hive Graph Search (PRD-vs-Corpus Conformance)
01KWM1G9Z4S77DYBSCBH1XN9ZK.description: This QA report audits PRD-012 against the Nectar knowledge corpus and Honeycomb code. It confirms the PRD's technical soundness, identifies minor documentation defects that were remediated, and reports substantive spec gaps for further consideration. The module passed with warnings.

01KWM1G9Z6GZS5T5JN1S4YBKF3.title: PRD-013: Recall Arm — Add `hive_graph_versions` to the Fused Recall
01KWM1G9Z6GZS5T5JN1S4YBKF3.description: This PRD integrates Nectar's file descriptions into Honeycomb's shared recall engine as a fourth arm. It details the process of adding a new arm for lexical and semantic search over `hive_graph_versions`, ensuring fail-soft behavior and seamless fusion with existing recall sources. This integration is crucial for agent-facing exposure to Nectar's data.

01KWM1G9Z862KZEQYWDMPV5YSF.title: PRD-013a: Lexical Arm — `buildHiveGraphVersionsArmSql` + `RecallSource` + `ARM_C
01KWM1G9Z862KZEQYWDMPV5YSF.description: This sub-PRD describes the implementation of the lexical arm for hive-graph recall, including the `buildHiveGraphVersionsArmSql` builder and its integration into the recall engine. It covers guarded SQL, `RecallSource` union updates, `ARM_CLASS_WEIGHT` for RRF fusion, and concurrent execution with other recall arms. The goal is a resilient search floor even without embeddings.

01KWM1G9Z97PRM5H6PPZSQ9Q46.title: PRD-013b: Semantic Arm — Cosine over the `embedding` Column
01KWM1G9Z97PRM5H6PPZSQ9Q46.description: This document specifies the implementation of the semantic recall arm for hive-graph data. It details how cosine similarity on the `embedding` column will be used to surface semantically relevant files, integrating into the existing hybrid recall system. The PRD focuses on reusing established vector search and hydration machinery without introducing new vector engines or changing embedding dimensionality.

01KWM1G9ZBC8XNSHTMD6B1ZNFY.title: PRD-013c: Graceful BM25-Only Fallback When Embeddings Are Off
01KWM1G9ZBC8XNSHTMD6B1ZNFY.description: This document ensures resilient recall behavior for hive-graph data when semantic embeddings are unavailable. It confirms that the lexical recall arm will function independently, providing a consistent fallback to BM25. The PRD emphasizes the fail-soft architecture, where missing dependencies or embedding failures do not result in errors but rather a graceful degradation to lexical-only recall.

01KWM1G9ZDHM78NB9MS36VWS07.title: QA Report: PRD-013 Recall Arm — `hive_graph_versions` (PRD-vs-Corpus-vs-Code Con
01KWM1G9ZDHM78NB9MS36VWS07.description: This QA report provides a comprehensive audit of PRD-013, covering the hive-graph recall arm. It verifies conformance against the knowledge corpus and existing code, identifying critical issues, warnings, and suggestions. The report focuses on SQL safety, adherence to fail-soft contracts, and traceability of acceptance criteria.

01KWM1G9ZFYJZC07XQ0VS2PCSP.title: PRD-014: Embeddings provider switching
01KWM1G9ZFYJZC07XQ0VS2PCSP.description: This document outlines the design for switching between different embeddings providers. It details the abstraction of embedding providers, allowing for a local nomic daemon as default and a Cohere-via-Portkey transport as an opt-in. The PRD emphasizes maintaining the 768-dim contract and extending existing configuration without new mechanisms.

01KWM1G9ZGHWRT13N9DC6DNR18.title: PRD-014a: Embed provider strategy and config
01KWM1G9ZGHWRT13N9DC6DNR18.description: This document defines the strategy and configuration for managing embedding providers. It establishes an `EmbedProvider` interface to abstract local nomic and Cohere-via-Portkey options, ensuring the local nomic daemon remains the default. The PRD focuses on extending existing configuration methods to enable seamless provider selection while preserving existing enable/disable semantics.

01KWM1G9ZKVRJPFC7T9SWS2TV4.title: PRD-014b: Cohere-via-Portkey embeddings transport
01KWM1G9ZKVRJPFC7T9SWS2TV4.description: This document specifies the Cohere-via-Portkey transport for embedding computation. It details reusing existing authentication patterns and host configurations from the rerank transport, ensuring the 768-dim contract is enforced. The PRD emphasizes fail-soft behavior and secret discipline, preventing errors and logging sensitive information.

01KWM1G9ZNQ1J83HMAE436CTA0.title: PRD-014c: Provider switch and BM25 fallback
01KWM1G9ZNQ1J83HMAE436CTA0.description: This document specifies the verification of embedding provider switching and the graceful fallback to BM25 search when embeddings are disabled or invalid. It ensures the system behaves as expected for different provider configurations and handles error conditions without disrupting recall functionality. The PRD outlines acceptance criteria for routing traffic to the correct provider and confirming the integrity of the BM25 fallback mechanism.

01KWM1G9ZQ6PDHYWPPHR48GCA3.title: QA Report: PRD-014 Embeddings Provider Switching (PRD-vs-Corpus/Code Conformance
01KWM1G9ZQ6PDHYWPPHR48GCA3.description: This QA report audits PRD-014 (Embeddings Provider Switching) against the Nectar knowledge corpus and Honeycomb code for conformance. It verifies that the PRD's specifications for provider switching, 768-dim contract, and BM25 fallback align with existing documentation and source code. The report provides a verdict scorecard, identifies critical issues, warnings, and suggestions, and traces acceptance criteria to their sources.

01KWM1G9ZSFZEM9D27BFWG7A13.title: PRD-015: Dashboard Hive Graph page
01KWM1G9ZSFZEM9D27BFWG7A13.description: This document outlines the requirements for adding a new 'Hive Graph' page to the dashboard. The page visualizes a file-provenance graph, a search box, and status/queue/cost widgets, all powered by Nectar API endpoints through Hive's aggregation layer. This PRD also details its sub-features and module-level acceptance criteria.

01KWM1G9ZV84KB74KNSGVC11E8.title: PRD-015a: Route registry entry + HiveGraphPage component
01KWM1G9ZV84KB74KNSGVC11E8.description: This sub-PRD focuses on integrating the Hive Graph page into the existing dashboard infrastructure. It defines the route entry in the shared ROUTES list and the `HiveGraphPage` component, ensuring consistent hydration via the shared `wire`/`usePoll` recipe. The document emphasizes reusing existing dashboard mechanisms for routing and data fetching.

01KWM1G9ZXDP3XFMD4M051HFVE.title: PRD-015b: File-graph visualization
01KWM1G9ZXDP3XFMD4M051HFVE.description: This sub-PRD details the visualization of the file graph within the Hive Graph page. It specifies that nodes represent individual files (nectars) and edges represent `derived_from_nectar` provenance links. The visualization reuses existing `GraphWire` structures and interaction primitives from the memory graph to ensure consistency and prevent bespoke development.

01KWM1G9ZZD5T58WTD132YZZ9R.title: PRD-015c: Search box + status widgets
01KWM1G9ZZD5T58WTD132YZZ9R.description: This sub-PRD addresses the implementation of a search box and status widgets on the Hive Graph page. The search functionality queries Nectar's hive-graph endpoints and drives node focus on the file graph. The status widgets display queue depth, description counts, and accumulated cost, all powered by Nectar's API through Hive's aggregation layer.

01KWM1GA01K555N60J5CB16TQ5.title: .gitkeep for PRD-015 Dashboard Hive Graph Page QA
01KWM1GA01K555N60J5CB16TQ5.description: This is a .gitkeep file, typically used to ensure that an otherwise empty directory is committed to Git. In this context, it guarantees the existence of the QA directory for the PRD-015 dashboard hive graph page.

01KWM1GA02MA9EXRA06F9JEGF6.title: QA Report: PRD-015 Dashboard Hive Graph Page (PRD-vs-Corpus-vs-Code Conformance)
01KWM1GA02MA9EXRA06F9JEGF6.description: This document provides a QA report for PRD-015, focusing on conformance between the product requirements document, the knowledge corpus, and the actual code. It details findings, warnings, and remediation actions for issues identified during the audit.

01KWM1GA05ATM3GWFG4HDPZJD2.title: PRD-016: Enricher Steady-State Loop + Meaningful Change Heuristic
01KWM1GA05ATM3GWFG4HDPZJD2.description: This document outlines the design for the Enricher's steady-state description-maintenance loop, which re-describes files upon meaningful content changes. It details the queue polling mechanism, watcher intake debounce, and a Jaccard similarity-based meaningful-change heuristic. The PRD also covers model calling, description auditing, and failure handling with persistent alerts.

01KWM1GA06T75TY9ZDZE2D6VKA.title: PRD-016a: Queue Poll, Debounce, and Meaningful-Change Heuristic
01KWM1GA06T75TY9ZDZE2D6VKA.description: This sub-PRD details the mechanisms by which the Enricher's steady-state loop efficiently processes file changes. It covers the 30-second queue poll that selects the latest pending version, the 500ms watcher intake debounce to coalesce rapid-fire saves, and the Jaccard similarity heuristic for identifying cosmetic versus meaningful content changes.

01KWM1GA083564E6DSKWNHZVGV.title: PRD-016b: Model Call and `describe_model` Audit
01KWM1GA083564E6DSKWNHZVGV.description: This sub-PRD specifies how the Enricher makes LLM calls to generate fresh descriptions for meaningful content changes. It defines the process for calling the model via Portkey, recording the producing model ID in the `describe_model` column, and managing cosmetic inheritance. The document also includes the computation of embeddings after description generation.

01KWM1GA0AQFBAZD916KGWDP36.title: PRD-016c: Failure Handling and Persistent-Failure Alert
01KWM1GA0AQFBAZD916KGWDP36.description: This sub-PRD outlines the Enricher's robust failure handling mechanisms for model calls. It details the retry-solo ladder for malformed or failed batches, the `describe_status = 'failed'` marking, and the persistent-failure alert that triggers after a default of 5 consecutive failed cycles. The goal is graceful degradation and operator notification for genuine outages.

01KWM1GA0BYR8FXK9H2YAS8F4X.title: QA Report: PRD-016 Enricher Steady-State Loop (PRD-vs-Corpus/Code Conformance)
01KWM1GA0BYR8FXK9H2YAS8F4X.description: This QA report provides a conformance audit of PRD-016 (Enricher Steady-State Loop) against Nectar's knowledge corpus and Honeycomb code. It traces all acceptance criteria and claims, identifying and remediating mechanical defects and flagging open warnings for further review. The report confirms strong alignment with documented standards and code.

01KWM1GA0DF7QNX6NEQBNM1M38.title: PRD-017: Nectar Service Check-in and SQLite Telemetry Emission
01KWM1GA0DF7QNX6NEQBNM1M38.description: This PRD defines how the Nectar service will integrate into the doctor-supervised fleet by registering itself and emitting non-sensitive telemetry. It covers extending the doctor registry writer, writing runtime status to a local SQLite database, and emitting metrics and logs using Node's built-in `node:sqlite`. The goal is to provide doctor with a pull-based, read-only telemetry surface.

01KWM1GA0EC1TTPS10PTDD9RWH.title: PRD-017a: Nectar Check-in and Registration for Telemetry
01KWM1GA0EC1TTPS10PTDD9RWH.description: This sub-PRD outlines how Nectar will extend its existing doctor registry entry to include the path to its runtime telemetry SQLite database. It details the process for Nectar to write and refresh a runtime status record, including binding time, last-seen heartbeat, and current health from its `/health` endpoint. All runtime status writes are designed to be fail-soft.

01KWM1GA0G412EYP1ZMJC1M3H2.title: PRD-017b: Nectar Metrics Emission to Local SQLite
01KWM1GA0G412EYP1ZMJC1M3H2.description: This sub-PRD specifies how Nectar will expose non-sensitive operational metrics to its local SQLite database for doctor to poll. It defines the `nectar_metrics` table structure for storing since-restart counters like files registered, nectars minted, and descriptions generated. The document emphasizes clean baselines on restart and fail-soft write operations.

01KWM1GA0J04QE1XV9SXQ6NJE7.title: PRD-017c: Nectar Log Emission to Local SQLite
01KWM1GA0J04QE1XV9SXQ6NJE7.description: This sub-PRD details the emission of Nectar's non-sensitive logs to a local SQLite database for doctor to poll. It defines the `nectar_logs` table for recent log lines with timestamps and verbosity levels, an enforced bounded and rotated retention policy, and redaction rules to prevent sensitive data exposure. All log writes are designed to be fail-soft.

01KWM1GA0MX4PMW81YZGXW49FP.title: Gitkeep for Service Check-in and SQLite Telemetry QA
01KWM1GA0MX4PMW81YZGXW49FP.description: This file is a placeholder to ensure the 'qa' directory for the service check-in and SQLite telemetry feature is included in version control. It prevents the directory from being omitted during Git operations.

01KWM1GA0PDT4PYD449J037EMP.title: QA Report: PRD-017 Service Check-in and SQLite Telemetry
01KWM1GA0PDT4PYD449J037EMP.description: This document is a retrospective QA report for PRD-017, covering service check-in and SQLite telemetry features. It details completeness, correctness, alignment, gaps, and detrimental aspects of the implementation, along with traceability to acceptance criteria. The report concludes with a 'PASS-with-warnings' verdict.

01KWM1GA0QG8TJJYRBX6A0C05F.title: Requirements — Completed PRDs
01KWM1GA0QG8TJJYRBX6A0C05F.description: This `README.md` file describes the purpose of the `completed` directory within the `library/requirements/` path. It states that this directory contains shipped Product Requirements Document (PRD) folders that are read-only and should not be modified after moving here from the `in-work/` directory. The file emphasizes that PRD indices, sub-PRDs, and QA sub-folders all move together into this completed state.

01KWM1GA0S1QC488PADX6RGEYS.title: PRD-018: Nectar pre-release close-out
01KWM1GA0S1QC488PADX6RGEYS.description: This document outlines PRD-018, a critical program to address 42 identified issues (NEC-001 through NEC-042) found during a pre-release review of the Nectar codebase. The program aims to make all three mission legs (brooding, update-on-change, recall) fully functional and verified by new integration tests. It details the program's scope, goals, non-goals, and traceability of issues to 12 sub-PRDs, along with a suggested execution order.

01KWM1GA0VNCPVP8C8XXWJ99CR.title: PRD-018a: Daemon lock and lifecycle correctness
01KWM1GA0VNCPVP8C8XXWJ99CR.description: This Product Requirements Document (PRD) outlines the critical fixes for daemon lock and lifecycle management. It addresses issues like non-atomic stale-lock reclaim, PID-reuse wedge, and shutdown hangs. The document defines goals for ownership-checked lock release, atomic reclaim, bounded shutdown, and single restart authority, with specific acceptance criteria and a list of files to be modified.

01KWM1GA0XM69ER6DZ2BCP6QN7.title: PRD-018b: Wire the update-on-change pipeline (mission leg 2)
01KWM1GA0XM69ER6DZ2BCP6QN7.description: This document outlines PRD-018b, focusing on making the 'update upon change' mission leg functional by wiring the `WatchIntake` and `RegistrationService` into the daemon. It addresses the issue of the pipeline being dead code in production and lack of cold catch-up on daemon start. The PRD specifies constructing the pipeline, bridging sync/async store interfaces, implementing cold catch-up, and unstubbing CLI verbs, with detailed acceptance criteria and affected files.

01KWM1GA0Y2FRRARXFH7TW6DPF.title: PRD-018c: Watcher robustness and ignore parity
01KWM1GA0Y2FRRARXFH7TW6DPF.description: This document outlines PRD-018c, focusing on enhancing the robustness and correctness of the file watcher system. It addresses inconsistencies in ignore rules between brooding and watching, silent dropping of directory renames, permanent watcher failures, and incorrect handling of case-only renames on case-insensitive filesystems. The PRD aims for a unified ignore predicate, event-driven reconciliation for renames, resilient watcher error recovery, and accurate stat refreshes to ensure an accurate and trustworthy observation layer.

01KWM1GA1051J5DC0MRR2AY6FM.title: PRD-018d: Re-association ladder correctness
01KWM1GA1051J5DC0MRR2AY6FM.description: This document specifies PRD-018d, focusing on critical fixes for the re-association ladder's correctness. It addresses issues where TLSH fuzzy matching mis-associates tiny files due to an inappropriate confidence mapping and where ladder actions are non-atomic, leading to data corruption on crashes. The PRD aims for size-aware fuzzy-match confidence, crash-consistent durable writes, and a reliable, bounded pending-review store that serializes concurrent writers and deduplicates candidates.

01KWM1GA128E84QSH9P27369SM.title: PRD-018e: Brooding durability and scale
01KWM1GA128E84QSH9P27369SM.description: This PRD outlines fixes for critical durability and scalability issues in the brooding process. It addresses the loss of LLM work on mid-run kill, excessive memory usage by retaining all file bytes, and failure to refresh changed files on resume. The goals are to ensure incremental persistence of described rows, bound memory usage by streaming and releasing buffers, and enable re-brooding of changed files.

01KWM1GA14QTEX48TEWATWF6MH.title: PRD-018f: Brooding batch-call robustness
01KWM1GA14QTEX48TEWATWF6MH.description: This document outlines PRD-018f, focusing on improving the robustness of brooding batch calls to the LLM. It addresses issues where batch failures lead to solo retry storms and excessive output token caps cause truncation and misattribution. The goals are to implement intelligent retry mechanisms for batch failures, size output token budgets appropriately, handle truncation by splitting batches, and ensure correct positional parsing to prevent description misattribution.

01KWM1GA16H07SDDW6GBH2JY5H.title: PRD-018g: Enricher correctness and concurrency
01KWM1GA16H07SDDW6GBH2JY5H.description: This document outlines PRD-018g, aiming to resolve six critical defects in the enricher's behavior related to concurrency, correctness, and output durability. It addresses races between brooding and enriching, `nextSeq` collisions, incorrect description attribution during batch processing, and the enricher's working set being frozen at boot. The PRD also targets ensuring durable write-backs, enabling the Jaccard cosmetic-change gate, and ensuring the projection regeneration trigger fires correctly.

01KWM1GA18N7YTXZANQ8DMVX00.title: PRD-018h: Recall ranking correctness and error honesty
01KWM1GA18N7YTXZANQ8DMVX00.description: This document addresses critical issues with recall ranking and error reporting in the semantic search system. It details problems with cosine distance interpretation, silent error swallowing, and non-deterministic lexical search results. The PRD outlines fixes to ensure accurate semantic ordering, honest degradation reporting, and consistent lexical search behavior.

01KWM1GA1AHJXTT2GAB9TDQXZB.title: PRD-018i: Embeddings and projection integrity
01KWM1GA1AHJXTT2GAB9TDQXZB.description: This document outlines fixes for four defects impacting the vector half of recall and projection integrity. It addresses issues like mixing incompatible embedding spaces, un-reembedded inherited rows, silent embedding dimension rejections, and duplicate-content file collapses. The goal is to ensure comparable embeddings, proper re-embedding, observable rejections, and unique identity preservation for all files.

01KWM1GA1CWMDAGF1Z009WCW78.title: PRD-018j: API security and doctor-registry hardening
01KWM1GA1CWMDAGF1Z009WCW78.description: This document details fixes for two security and hardening gaps: the daemon's API tenancy override and the non-atomic doctor-registry file writes. It aims to prevent unauthorized cross-project data access or modification through the API and ensure durable, non-lossy updates to the daemon's registration file. The solutions involve tightening API access controls and implementing atomic file write operations.

01KWM1GA1DBNMHM64FP460W6K6.title: PRD-018k: First-run experience and the config file
01KWM1GA1DBNMHM64FP460W6K6.description: This document addresses two critical issues impacting the new user experience: silent brooding dormancy and an unimplemented repository configuration file. It aims to make daemon startup failures due to missing prerequisites immediately obvious and to implement the promised per-repository configuration loader. The goal is a smoother and more transparent first-run experience for users.

01KWM1GA1FYMG5DB099E126W0C.title: PRD-018l: Docs truth pass and low-severity cleanup batch
01KWM1GA1FYMG5DB099E126W0C.description: This document describes a comprehensive truth pass for all public documentation and a batch fix for fourteen low-severity issues. It focuses on ensuring all command examples work as written, resolving internal documentation contradictions, and addressing small, self-contained defects. The goal is to align documentation with the shipped product and enhance overall system polish and reliability.

01KWM1GA1GVF5ME3MPT8V5QNBY.title: QA Report: PRD-018 Pre-Release Close-out
01KWM1GA1GVF5ME3MPT8V5QNBY.description: This QA report summarizes the independent audit of the PRD-018 Pre-Release Close-out epic. It details the re-verification of all initial critical and warning findings, confirming remediation and overall project readiness. The report concludes with a PASS verdict, highlighting successful live Deep Lake tests, `embed_model` heal, and documentation command validation.

01KWM1GA1HZD7DHDVZ50EBEF5R.title: In-Work Requirements (README)
01KWM1GA1HZD7DHDVZ50EBEF5R.description: This file describes the 'in-work' directory for Product Requirements Documents (PRDs). It contains PRD folders that are actively being implemented, moving from backlog/ when work begins and to completed/ when shipping.

01KWM1GA1KK50WKRBZ4N0SSGFQ.title: Master PRD Index — Nectar Implementation
01KWM1GA1KK50WKRBZ4N0SSGFQ.description: This document serves as a master index for all Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) related to the Nectar implementation project. It outlines the ordered list of PRDs, their status, dependencies, and key architectural decisions made, serving as a central reference for the project's requirements and progress.

01KWM1GA1M1MZZHM6CCEMNVXY9.title: Nectar Pre-Release Issues (2026-07-02)
01KWM1GA1M1MZZHM6CCEMNVXY9.description: This document compiles pre-release issues identified in the Nectar project as of July 2, 2026. It categorizes them by criticality (Critical, High, Medium, Low) and outlines specific problems found, providing a consolidated list for resolution ahead of release.

01KWM1GA1PN2DNS55JKGYD98X3.title: PRD-003 to PRD-017 Dependency Map (Nectar Implementation)
01KWM1GA1PN2DNS55JKGYD98X3.description: This document details the dependencies between Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) for the Nectar implementation project, covering PRD-003 through PRD-017. It provides a comprehensive map for execution drivers to schedule work, indicating upstream dependencies, downstream dependents, and blockers for each PRD and sub-PRD.

01KWM1GA1RM1KZSHNGXVNRVQS8.title: PRD-003 to PRD-017 Wave Plan (Nectar Implementation)
01KWM1GA1RM1KZSHNGXVNRVQS8.description: This document outlines the phased 'wave plan' for the Nectar implementation, sequencing PRDs from 003 to 017 across three repositories. It defines entry and exit gates for each wave, specifies parallel and sequential tasks, and guides the execution process based on a topological sort of PRD dependencies.

01KWM1GA1SJ0HVHMTNZ89WCGXF.title: PRD Decisions and Defaults
01KWM1GA1SJ0HVHMTNZ89WCGXF.description: This document consolidates key decisions and default settings for the 17 Nectar Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) for review and confirmation. It lists previously locked decisions for traceability, identifies defaults requiring confirmation before implementation, and addresses corpus inconsistencies found during QA.

01KWM1GA1V04B9TJJN3WZKXES6.title: Requirements (README)
01KWM1GA1V04B9TJJN3WZKXES6.description: This file describes the structure and lifecycle management for Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) within this repository. It outlines how PRD folders are organized into 'backlog', 'in-work', and 'completed' states, defining naming conventions and a folder-based lifecycle model.

01KWM1GA1WZKG3ZGDRCF577X9T.title: Corpus Conformance QA Report: PRD-001 to PRD-004
01KWM1GA1WZKG3ZGDRCF577X9T.description: This report details a consolidated quality assurance audit of the first four Nectar Product Requirement Documents (PRD-001 through PRD-004) against the knowledge corpus. It verifies acceptance criteria and load-bearing descriptions against their cited sources. The audit confirms all four modules PASS with minor warnings and observations, ensuring strong grounding of PRDs in the knowledge base.

01KWM1GA1Y1HCWN5DDMNXA5PNV.title: Requirements Reports README
01KWM1GA1Y1HCWN5DDMNXA5PNV.description: This document describes the convention for routine code-scan, QA, and security reports within the requirements directory. It specifies naming conventions for these reports and indicates where specific PRD or IRD related QA reports should be located. This helps maintain an organized structure for various audits and scans.

01KWM1GA22EG1B9G8QKE2PPS54.title: package-lock.json for Nectar project
01KWM1GA22EG1B9G8QKE2PPS54.description: This file is the `package-lock.json` for the '@legioncodeinc/nectar' project. It records the exact versions of dependencies that were installed. This ensures consistent installs across different environments.

01KWM1GA24GXFWYP9PNE5H91EV.title: package.json for Nectar project
01KWM1GA24GXFWYP9PNE5H91EV.description: This is the `package.json` file for the '@legioncodeinc/nectar' project, defining its metadata, dependencies, and scripts. It describes 'Nectar' as a semantic memory layer over a source tree, minting identities and LLM-generated descriptions for files. It also configures publishing settings for the package.

01KWM1GA267698912GRVM1JV3R.title: Script to Bake PostHog Key into Compiled Output
01KWM1GA267698912GRVM1JV3R.description: This Node.js script dynamically injects PostHog ingest keys and host information into the compiled `dist/telemetry-usage/posthog-key.js` file at release time. It handles environmental variables for the keys and verifies that the baked values round-trip correctly. This allows for telemetry configuration without hardcoding secrets in source.

01KWM1GA27YHXF4X2KHBBWP4E4.title: Daemon API Wiring for Hive Graph
01KWM1GA27YHXF4X2KHBBWP4E4.description: This module provides the production wiring for the `/api/hive-graph` handlers, configuring how the Nectar daemon interacts with the Hive Graph and its dependencies. It dynamically sets up search, brood (build), and projection functionalities based on daemon configuration and available credentials. Key components include DeepLake transport, embedding providers, and robust error handling.

01KWM1GA295MMEZJHD88XN7NDN.title: API for Hive Graph Daemon
01KWM1GA295MMEZJHD88XN7NDN.description: This module defines the API handlers for the `/api/hive-graph` group, mirroring Honeycomb's router pattern. It includes endpoints for search, build (brood), status, and projection management. The implementation ensures that all API interactions honor tenancy scope and provide structured error responses.

01KWM1GA2ADGT2G17JV2RVY52W.title: Loopback Client for Nectar Search
01KWM1GA2ADGT2G17JV2RVY52W.description: This module implements a thin loopback HTTP client for the `nectar search` command-line interface. It communicates with the running daemon over `127.0.0.1:3854` and does not import the search engine directly. This design minimizes the client's runtime footprint and ensures it reflects the daemon's live state or gracefully reports unreachability.

01KWM1GA2CKZ3S2GKF5TFXW4WX.title: Nectar In-Repo Router
01KWM1GA2CKZ3S2GKF5TFXW4WX.description: This module provides Nectar's minimal, zero-runtime-dependency router over `node:http`, mirroring Honeycomb's router patterns. It defines route groups, handles request dispatching, enforces maximum body sizes, and manages permission gates. This custom router ensures lightweight and consistent API handling within the Nectar daemon.

01KWM1GA2D4QFBGRPSPGW6R5HM.title: API Hive Graph Status Query
01KWM1GA2D4QFBGRPSPGW6R5HM.description: Defines the API endpoint for retrieving the coarse pipeline status of the Hive Graph. This includes queue depth, `describe_status` breakdown, and cumulative cost, designed for operator dashboards and health checks. It gracefully handles missing tables by reporting a degraded status rather than failing.

01KWM1GA2FDK86H2WXHEZCCH4B.title: Brood Guard
01KWM1GA2FDK86H2WXHEZCCH4B.description: Provides a shared in-process guard to ensure that only one 'brood' operation runs at a time within the daemon. It prevents concurrent brood processes and allows the enricher to pause when a brood is active. This module helps manage concurrency for critical pipeline stages.

01KWM1GA2HZDXMP5N7HSXNJYF0.title: Brood Prerequisite Evaluation and Guidance
01KWM1GA2HZDXMP5N7HSXNJYF0.description: Handles the evaluation of prerequisites for brooding operations, such as Deep Lake credentials and Portkey configuration. It provides machine-readable dormancy reasons for health checks and human-readable guidance for command-line users on how to resolve missing prerequisites. This ensures clear communication about why brooding isn't active.

01KWM1GA2KECW0NYRFZJWE1JQS.title: Brooding Pipeline Bucketing and Batch Packing
01KWM1GA2KECW0NYRFZJWE1JQS.description: Implements the bucketing and dynamic batch packing logic for files in the brooding pipeline. Files are classified into `skip-binary`, `skip-too-large`, `batch`, and `solo` categories based on size and content. The batch packing dynamically groups files to optimize API calls while respecting token budgets and size limits.

01KWM1GA2M44971ZCC2PDNZZXF.title: Brooding Pipeline CLI Surface
01KWM1GA2M44971ZCC2PDNZZXF.description: Defines the command-line interface for the `brood` verb, including flag parsing (`--force`, `--dry-run`, `--limit`, `--model`). It also implements the logic for automatic brooding triggers and formatting dry-run cost previews. This module centralizes the CLI interactions for the brooding process.

01KWM1GA2PNRAF4Z123BK7DYB4.title: Brooding Pipeline Constants and Thresholds
01KWM1GA2PNRAF4Z123BK7DYB4.description: Centralizes all fundamental thresholds, bucket definitions, and batch-packing constants for the brooding pipeline. These values, such as file size limits, token budgets, and known binary extensions, are directly sourced from the project's design documentation. This module ensures consistency across the brooding process's various stages.

01KWM1GA2QFZXFA636T7T8EH26.title: Brooding Cost Calculations
01KWM1GA2QFZXFA636T7T8EH26.description: This module estimates the cost of brooding files based on Gemini 2.5 Flash pricing. It includes reference costs for various repository sizes and calculates costs for specific projects using their bucketed file counts.

01KWM1GA2SX7Q0CBXQDS7KYD4H.title: Brooding Describe and Embed Stages
01KWM1GA2SX7Q0CBXQDS7KYD4H.description: This module handles the 'describe' and 'embed' stages of the brooding pipeline. It defines prompts, parses model responses, and manages the embedding process for file descriptions.

01KWM1GA2TJKEF9HZGQW8ADN6E.title: Brooding File Discovery
01KWM1GA2TJKEF9HZGQW8ADN6E.description: This module discovers files for brooding, primarily using 'git ls-files'. It falls back to a manual recursive walk with CodeGraph's ignore contract if Git is unavailable or errors.

01KWM1GA2WHSX896T16WED4DWF.title: Brooding Module Barrel File
01KWM1GA2WHSX896T16WED4DWF.description: This file serves as the main entry point for the brooding module, exporting all its public interfaces. It orchestrates the fixed pipeline stages for file discovery, pre-checking, bucketing, description, embedding, and persistence.

01KWM1GA2XTT2RPCY767R7C1JN.description: This module provides an asynchronous version of the brooding pipeline, designed to work with async-native data stores like DeepLake. It reuses store-agnostic stages from the synchronous pipeline but handles store interactions asynchronously.

01KWM1GA2ZXG097MEEDV4MHYPN.title: Brooding Pipeline Orchestrator
01KWM1GA2ZXG097MEEDV4MHYPN.description: This module orchestrates the entire brooding pipeline, from file discovery to projection regeneration. It handles resumability, manages LLM calls, embeds descriptions, and persists data incrementally.

01KWM1GA30YXM0ZFC200XWSEA1.title: Brooding Content Hash Pre-check
01KWM1GA30YXM0ZFC200XWSEA1.description: This module performs a content-hash pre-check, the second stage of the brooding pipeline. It identifies files whose content hash matches an existing projection, inheriting their descriptions and skipping LLM calls for them.

01KWM1GA314FY6XXN1GHRFD394.title: Brooding Resumability State Machine
01KWM1GA314FY6XXN1GHRFD394.description: This module implements the resumability state machine for the brooding pipeline. It determines whether a file should be skipped, re-enqueued, or discovered fresh based on its latest description status and content hash.

01KWM1GA3389E0TC22C9M6VDX5.title: Nectar CLI Entry Point
01KWM1GA3389E0TC22C9M6VDX5.description: This file provides the command-line interface for the Nectar semantic memory system. It handles various commands like `daemon`, `install`, `brood`, `search`, `prune`, `review-matches`, and `rebuild-projection`, interacting with daemon services and durable stores.

01KWM1GA340G01A2YFTVTPZH1K.title: Nectar Per-Repo Config Loader
01KWM1GA340G01A2YFTVTPZH1K.description: This module loads and resolves configuration tunables from `~/.honeycomb/nectar.json`. It applies precedence rules where environment variables take priority over file-based settings, ensuring fail-soft operation for malformed files.

01KWM1GA36ETS6QBFKMNN2KCGE.title: Nectar Runtime Configuration
01KWM1GA36ETS6QBFKMNN2KCGE.description: This module resolves the runtime configuration for the Nectar daemon, defining host, port, polling interval, and file paths. It prioritizes explicit overrides, then environment variables, then built-in defaults.

01KWM1GA374HFNW24YEB6NQ9N5.title: Nectar Daemon Composition Root
01KWM1GA374HFNW24YEB6NQ9N5.description: This file defines the Nectar daemon's core lifecycle, mirroring Honeycomb's daemon architecture. It handles daemon assembly, startup logic, shutdown procedures, and integrates various components like health checks, telemetry, and the enrichment pipeline.

01KWM1GA39H88G9W3CTP4MPP98.title: Doctor Daemon Registry Integration
01KWM1GA39H88G9W3CTP4MPP98.description: This module handles Nectar's registration with Doctor's daemon registry, appending a configuration entry to `~/.honeycomb/doctor.daemons.json`. It specifies Nectar's health URL, PID path, probe intervals, and telemetry database path, allowing Doctor to supervise and monitor it.

01KWM1GA3ASHAKNZWMZP4Z5VNC.title: Embeddings Configuration Resolver
01KWM1GA3ASHAKNZWMZP4Z5VNC.description: This file resolves the configuration for Nectar's embedding providers, supporting 'off', 'local' (Nomic), and 'hosted' (Portkey) options. It uses a precedence system (overrides > environment variables > defaults) and handles legacy boolean environment variable mappings.

01KWM1GA3CDWH98S5STGTPK6G5.title: Embedding Dimension Guard
01KWM1GA3CDWH98S5STGTPK6G5.description: This module implements a critical 768-dimension guard for embedding vectors, ensuring data integrity within the `FLOAT4[768]` column schema. It discards wrong-dimension vectors, replacing them with `null`, and reports rejections without impacting the embedding pipeline.

01KWM1GA3EK1VF627P9N7BQB2M.title: Hosted Portkey Embeddings Provider
01KWM1GA3EK1VF627P9N7BQB2M.description: This module provides the hosted embeddings transport via Portkey, utilizing OpenAI's `text-embedding-3-small` model. It handles API key authentication, request/response parsing, and implements a fail-soft, bounded retry mechanism for network and API errors.

01KWM1GA3F70HFSAYQAW56DDHQ.title: HTTP Abstractions for Embeddings
01KWM1GA3F70HFSAYQAW56DDHQ.description: This file defines minimal HTTP abstractions (`FetchResponseLike`, `FetchLike`, `SleepFn`) for Nectar's embedding transports. These injectable interfaces allow for easy mocking during unit testing, preventing actual network requests.

01KWM1GA3GA5AVF4ZQZ092G4BP.title: Embeddings Module Barrel
01KWM1GA3GA5AVF4ZQZ092G4BP.description: This file serves as the barrel for Nectar's embeddings provider module, re-exporting all public interfaces and utility functions. It simplifies imports for integrating embeddings logic into other parts of the daemon, such as the enricher or brooding components.

01KWM1GA3JH0QQ64X7X0KMK3P5.title: Local Nomic Embeddings Provider
01KWM1GA3JH0QQ64X7X0KMK3P5.description: This module implements the local Nomic embeddings provider, communicating with a local `nomic-embed-text-v1.5` daemon over loopback HTTP. It handles text embedding requests, parses responses, and ensures fail-soft behavior for various connectivity or response issues.

01KWM1GA3KVKC8Y6MZG8S2JTJ7.title: Embeddings Provider Strategy Switch
01KWM1GA3KVKC8Y6MZG8S2JTJ7.description: This module implements the strategy switch for Nectar's embedding providers, toggling between 'off', 'local' (Nomic), and 'hosted' (Portkey) options. It ensures all computing providers are wrapped with a 768-dimension guard for data consistency.

01KWM1GA3NXRW3R3E0WVD14TKK.title: Enricher Configuration Tunables
01KWM1GA3NXRW3R3E0WVD14TKK.description: This file defines and resolves tunable configuration parameters for Nectar's enricher component, such as poll intervals, redescribe thresholds, and batch sizes. It prioritizes explicit overrides, then tunables from configuration files/environment, and finally signed-off defaults.

01KWM1GA3P4Q27RK2T71Y59YCN.description: Implements a bounded, in-process cache for prior file content. This cache is used by the enricher to determine cosmetic changes via Jaccard similarity without re-describing the content.

01KWM1GA3QEXBCHFVTD5JWFSX0.title: Enricher Cycle Logic
01KWM1GA3QEXBCHFVTD5JWFSX0.description: This file defines a single cycle of the Nectar enricher, including working-set refreshment, brood coordination, content selection, cosmetic inheritance checking, embedding generation, describing content, and durable storage updates. It handles batch processing, error management, and projection regeneration triggers.

01KWM1GA3RFT9QG8E9WDBAXD65.title: Enricher Description Logic
01KWM1GA3RFT9QG8E9WDBAXD65.description: This module handles the core logic for describing source files using an LLM, including prompt construction, response parsing, and error handling. It defines interfaces for file input and batch results, and implements mechanisms to ensure robust and efficient LLM interactions.

01KWM1GA3TVTQC2G98X3XJFJ52.title: Enricher Failure Management
01KWM1GA3TVTQC2G98X3XJFJ52.description: Defines interfaces and functions for managing the enricher's persistent failure state. It tracks consecutive failures, raises alerts, and supports acknowledging alerts to prevent enrichment from halting indefinitely.

01KWM1GA3V3JDHVK669F6TX0NA.title: Enricher Module Exports
01KWM1GA3V3JDHVK669F6TX0NA.description: This file serves as the public API for the Nectar enricher module, re-exporting various components and utilities. It includes configuration options, tokenization functions, Jaccard similarity calculations, content transformation logic, store interfaces, and the core enricher cycle functions.

01KWM1GA3WQTRR75TS509SJC2A.title: Jaccard Similarity Calculation
01KWM1GA3WQTRR75TS509SJC2A.description: Calculates the Jaccard similarity between two multisets of tokens, primarily used to compare source code content. This helps determine if a change is 'meaningful' or 'cosmetic' within the enricher.

01KWM1GA3YV3ZR6XYS6AG5PEAB.title: Enricher Poll Loop
01KWM1GA3YV3ZR6XYS6AG5PEAB.description: Implements the core polling mechanism for the enricher, managing its execution cadence and interaction with failure state. It orchestrates the `runEnricherCycle` function within a recurring loop.

01KWM1GA3ZC2VNNRQ3DP541TM0.title: Meaningful Change Heuristic and Inheritance
01KWM1GA3ZC2VNNRQ3DP541TM0.description: Determines if a content change is 'cosmetic' (allowing description inheritance) or 'meaningful' (requiring a new description). Provides logic to build and apply cosmetic inheritance patches for files that haven't meaningfully changed.

01KWM1GA425F93767HC1H704RF.title: Enricher Cycle Observability
01KWM1GA425F93767HC1H704RF.description: Defines data structures and functions for tracking and logging enricher cycle statistics. This includes metrics like files described, token counts, estimated costs, and queue depth.

01KWM1GA44TH4CWC4PDKWRREVD.title: Pending Work SQL Query Builder
01KWM1GA44TH4CWC4PDKWRREVD.description: Constructs SQL queries to retrieve pending or failed work items for the enricher. It ensures that only the latest version of a file is considered and respects tenancy boundaries.

01KWM1GA45C23JP9X54M25M0XY.title: Enricher SQL UPDATE Builder
01KWM1GA45C23JP9X54M25M0XY.description: Generates SQL UPDATE statements for modifying `hive_graph_versions` table rows. It handles various data types and ensures proper escaping for database interaction.

01KWM1GA46DMD2CT499CDXN8RE.title: Deep Lake Enricher Store Adapter
01KWM1GA46DMD2CT499CDXN8RE.description: Adapts an asynchronous durable store (Deep Lake) to the synchronous `EnricherStore` interface using an in-memory mirror. It handles version-bump appends for described rows and periodic mirror refreshes.

01KWM1GA470JTDTCSYFJRYW4KC.title: Enricher Store Interface and In-Memory Implementation
01KWM1GA470JTDTCSYFJRYW4KC.description: Defines the interface for the enricher's data store, including methods for listing pending work, retrieving versions, and committing described rows. It also provides an in-memory implementation for testing and development.

01KWM1GA49C055XVF5EWBXPCZB.title: Tokenization for Change Heuristics
01KWM1GA49C055XVF5EWBXPCZB.description: This file provides utilities for tokenizing source code into a stream of meaningful tokens. It's used for the 'meaningful change' heuristic (PRD-016a), enabling Jaccard comparison of code changes.

01KWM1GA4ACY7ZPV4AJNRX7FDX.title: Nectar Daemon Errors
01KWM1GA4ACY7ZPV4AJNRX7FDX.description: Defines custom error types specific to the Nectar daemon's operation. These errors handle scenarios like a daemon already running, startup abortions, configuration issues, and insecure API bindings.

01KWM1GA4BYX4GREAK07BD0MWD.title: Nectar Daemon Health Status
01KWM1GA4BYX4GREAK07BD0MWD.description: Defines the health contract for the nectar daemon, including a structured body with status information for brooding, enricher, projection, cost, embeddings, and watch subsystems. It allows subsystems to report their state and supports a coarse `ok`/`degraded` status.

01KWM1GA4D6Z8RRTMCYFNGXF1J.title: Deep Lake Credentials Loader
01KWM1GA4D6Z8RRTMCYFNGXF1J.description: Handles loading and validating Deep Lake credentials from a shared `~/.deeplake/credentials.json` file. It ensures secure and robust access to Deep Lake by validating required fields and providing warnings for insecure file permissions.

01KWM1GA4ER04HQP45C0EQEW9Q.title: Deep Lake Heal-on-Write Mechanism
01KWM1GA4ER04HQP45C0EQEW9Q.description: Implements a heal-on-first-write mechanism for the Deep Lake adapter, enabling automatic creation of missing tables or addition of missing columns when a write operation fails due to their absence. This ensures schema evolution without manual intervention, supporting additive changes only.

01KWM1GA4GHM3BE3729BHAC9Y8.title: DeepLakeHiveGraphStore: Deep Lake-backed HiveGraphStore adapter
01KWM1GA4GHM3BE3729BHAC9Y8.description: This module provides a Deep Lake-backed adapter for the Hive Graph store, implementing the AsyncHiveGraphStore interface. It handles data persistence for hive_graph and hive_graph_versions tables, accessed over HTTP. The store manages identity and version rows, with specific logic for sequence allocation, version reduction, and tenancy-scoped operations.

01KWM1GA4H03MEGGX0CPGSS71D.title: Deep Lake HTTP Transport for Nectar
01KWM1GA4H03MEGGX0CPGSS71D.description: This module provides a minimal HTTP transport layer for interacting with Deep Lake from Nectar. It handles posting SQL statements to the Deep Lake query endpoint and manages basic error handling, including retries for transient failures. This transport is designed to be thin and self-contained, using only Node.js built-ins.

01KWM1GA4JX7XJ7R1T4R7HRHRS.title: Content Hashing for Hive Graph
01KWM1GA4JX7XJ7R1T4R7HRHRS.description: This module provides utility functions for content hashing within the Hive Graph system. It specifically implements SHA256 hashing for file content, used to generate content_hash values for versioning and indexing. The implementation uses Node.js's built-in crypto module.

01KWM1GA4MQ31YRMVGKFDWYQ07.title: InMemoryHiveGraphStore: In-memory HiveGraphStore adapter
01KWM1GA4MQ31YRMVGKFDWYQ07.description: This module provides an in-memory implementation of the HiveGraphStore interface using JavaScript Maps. It's used for testing and local development, offering a complete and correct model of the store's behavior, including tenancy-scoped operations and version management. This in-memory store serves as a reference for the Deep Lake adapter.

01KWM1GA4N1SN86B9YAFDBHA70.title: Hive Graph Domain Model
01KWM1GA4N1SN86B9YAFDBHA70.description: This module defines the core domain model for the Hive Graph, encompassing data structures for file identities, provenance, and versioned file content. It includes interfaces for `HiveGraphRow` and `HiveGraphVersionRow`, along with types and constants for `DescribeStatus`, `NectarKind`, and `Tenancy`. The schema directly reflects the underlying Deep Lake table structures.

01KWM1GA4PTMSK2HYD3N3G1K0Q.title: Path Helpers for Hive Graph
01KWM1GA4PTMSK2HYD3N3G1K0Q.description: This module provides utility functions for manipulating file paths within the Hive Graph system. It offers methods to convert paths to a repo-relative, forward-slashed format, extract filenames, and determine file extensions. These helpers ensure consistent path representation across different operating systems for storage and searching.

01KWM1GA4RXTZ4MRM5VW58C73C.title: Project Scope Resolution for Nectar
01KWM1GA4RXTZ4MRM5VW58C73C.description: This module resolves the project scope for a given Nectar invocation based on a defined precedence ladder. It considers environment variables, cached Deep Lake project bindings, and Git remote signals, falling back to an '__unsorted__' inbox if no specific project is found. The resolution is a pure function, ensuring consistency across concurrent sessions.

01KWM1GA4SMQZDJ6G2J7P3398Z.title: Hive Graph Schema Definitions
01KWM1GA4SMQZDJ6G2J7P3398Z.description: Defines the schema for `hive_graph` and `hive_graph_versions` tables, including column definitions, data types, and write patterns. It also provides utilities for building SQL `CREATE TABLE` and `ALTER TABLE` statements for Deep Lake.

01KWM1GA4VF74Q8BC2RCADCBKP.description: Defines the TypeScript types for the hive-graph search engine, including interfaces for storage queries, embedding clients, search results, and dependency injection. It outlines the structure of search hits, arm statuses, and reasons for degradation.

01KWM1GA4WBG2BVANS1WM87MHT.title: Hive Graph Search Engine
01KWM1GA4WBG2BVANS1WM87MHT.description: Implements a standalone search engine for the hive graph, combining lexical (ILIKE) and semantic (vector) search arms. It includes functions for building SQL queries, fusing results using RRF, and handling dependency injection for storage and embedding services.

01KWM1GA4XVVMTF9FRNK3DM2JR.title: SQL Safety Guards
01KWM1GA4XVVMTF9FRNK3DM2JR.description: Provides SQL-safety escaping helper functions to prevent injection attacks when building Deep Lake queries. It includes functions for escaping string literals, LIKE patterns, identifiers, and numeric/float array values.

01KWM1GA4ZTTGB9ZG69MVXBHV4.title: Hive Graph Store Interfaces
01KWM1GA4ZTTGB9ZG69MVXBHV4.description: Defines synchronous (`HiveGraphStore`) and asynchronous (`AsyncHiveGraphStore`) interfaces for interacting with the Hive Graph data layer. These interfaces specify methods for managing identity rows, version rows, and querying the graph, catering to both in-memory and durable (Deep Lake) implementations.

01KWM1GA5052JFG90VERZ8JE5M.title: ULID Minting for Nectars
01KWM1GA5052JFG90VERZ8JE5M.description: Provides functions for minting and validating Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifiers (ULIDs) for nectars. It ensures monotonic generation within the same millisecond and offers utilities for decoding timestamps and checking format validity.

01KWM1GA52Q8FKXY6CWGW8CZ22.title: Nectar Public Entry Point
01KWM1GA52Q8FKXY6CWGW8CZ22.description: The public entry point for the `@legioncodeinc/nectar` package, re-exporting key modules and types for programmatic use. It provides access to daemon assembly, configuration, lock management, API routing, Hive Graph data layer, registration protocol, and telemetry.

01KWM1GA53RXNMFG6DCNXEYYHQ.title: Single-Instance PID/Lock Guard
01KWM1GA53RXNMFG6DCNXEYYHQ.description: Implements a robust single-instance lock mechanism for the nectar daemon using PID files and lock files. It prevents multiple daemon instances from running concurrently, atomically reclaims stale locks, and provides ownership-checked release to avoid accidental deletions.

01KWM1GA54X6BA8FPA55XV3N8V.title: Adaptive Polling Loop
01KWM1GA54X6BA8FPA55XV3N8V.description: Implements an adaptive polling loop with backoff, ensuring only one tick runs at a time. It mirrors honeycomb's poll-loop, designed for deterministic behavior under manual clock in tests.

01KWM1GA552DQP8X74DRVXZ7GY.title: Portkey Configuration Resolver
01KWM1GA552DQP8X74DRVXZ7GY.description: Defines and resolves configurations for Portkey API access, including API key, configuration ID, and the active model for description. It supports environment variables and explicit overrides for flexible setup.

01KWM1GA575PN17X9XRRKSYAN0.title: Describe Model Audit Seam
01KWM1GA575PN17X9XRRKSYAN0.description: Manages the audit stamping for description models on Hive Graph versions and identifies rows that need to be reset for re-description. It defines statuses for skipped descriptions and structures for description payloads and model stamps.

01KWM1GA5815SBQPRE3E3740ZY.title: Portkey API Headers and Endpoints
01KWM1GA5815SBQPRE3E3740ZY.description: Specifies constants for Portkey API base URL, chat completions, and embeddings endpoints, along with helper functions to build necessary request headers including API key and configuration ID. This mirrors honeycomb's internal Portkey integration.

01KWM1GA5ADA3ZR9EYRBGD9SWA.title: Portkey Chat Completions Transport
01KWM1GA5ADA3ZR9EYRBGD9SWA.description: Provides a client for making chat completions requests via Portkey, including retry logic for transient failures and controlled timeouts. It handles request serialization, response parsing, and error handling for interacting with the Portkey gateway.

01KWM1GA5B6YBTS78CPX82ZZD8.title: Portable Projection Document Format
01KWM1GA5B6YBTS78CPX82ZZD8.description: Defines the schema and canonical serialization for portable projection documents, which represent the state of described files and derived content. It includes utilities for validating content hashes and deterministic JSON serialization.

01KWM1GA5CVJ267D3X63ZP0PY6.title: Projection Document Generation
01KWM1GA5CVJ267D3X63ZP0PY6.description: Generates portable projection documents from Hive Graph store state, aggregating described file versions and derived content. It offers both synchronous and asynchronous store adapters to build the projection.

01KWM1GA5D7F4A16HBH34S713J.title: Projection Inheritance Logic
01KWM1GA5D7F4A16HBH34S713J.description: Implements the logic for inheriting descriptions and metadata from a validated projection document based on content hash matching. It generates new Hive Graph rows for hash-matched files, respecting existing local nectars and handling content duplicates.

01KWM1GA5GY4H4Q2H8DJ7Z9KEE.title: Projection Store Adapter
01KWM1GA5GY4H4Q2H8DJ7Z9KEE.description: This module provides an adapter layer for generating projections from a HiveGraph store. It defines how to collect nectar and version information, prioritizing described versions for the projection output.

01KWM1GA5H290KSAX3D0QVWNCP.title: Atomic Projection Writer with Debounce
01KWM1GA5H290KSAX3D0QVWNCP.description: This utility provides atomic writing of projection documents to disk, ensuring data consistency even during crashes. It also includes a debounced writer for coalescing rapid write operations, improving performance during frequent updates.

01KWM1GA5JH7D7766Y4NWB0TC3.title: Path Observation Classifier
01KWM1GA5JH7D7766Y4NWB0TC3.description: This module classifies file system path observations into categories for a re-association ladder. It determines if a path is new, changed, or missing, based on its existence and prior knowledge, handling case-insensitive comparisons where necessary.

01KWM1GA5MY50P1KXTRCGQBSB9.title: New File Copy Detection
01KWM1GA5MY50P1KXTRCGQBSB9.description: This module determines if a newly observed file is genuinely new or a copy of an existing one. It uses content hashes to detect copy events, allowing for proper attribution and derivation in the graph.

01KWM1GA5NEQNK6KFH67SAMWYY.title: Disk-based Registration File System
01KWM1GA5NEQNK6KFH67SAMWYY.description: This module provides a concrete implementation of `RegistrationFs` using the actual disk. It ensures secure path containment and handles symlinks and directory ignores during file system operations.

01KWM1GA5PB7HDQGCAHPZ7E6FF.title: File Watch Intake with Debounce and Restart
01KWM1GA5PB7HDQGCAHPZ7E6FF.description: This module wraps Node.js's file system watcher, providing debounced path observations and full resync requests. It also implements robust error handling with exponential backoff and state management for watcher liveness.

01KWM1GA5R6RV963C6J17PCNY0.title: Workspace Ignore Predicate
01KWM1GA5R6RV963C6J17PCNY0.description: This module defines a shared ignore contract for the file registration intake, combining built-in rules, per-repo ignore files, and gitignore semantics. It provides mechanisms to refresh the gitignore snapshot and report errors.

01KWM1GA5TKW2ZPRWT3MX8XM4S.title: Ladder for File Re-association (PRD-006d)
01KWM1GA5TKW2ZPRWT3MX8XM4S.description: Describes the 5-step re-association ladder for files, handling identity, versioning, and provenance. It defines how new and changed files are classified and processed, including fuzzy matching and change detection.

01KWM1GA5WY4CFDN1D2HK6HNCV.title: Workspace Path Containment Utilities
01KWM1GA5WY4CFDN1D2HK6HNCV.description: Provides utilities to ensure file paths are safe and contained within a specified workspace root. It performs lexical checks for absolute paths and '..' traversals, and a realpath check for symlink escapes.

01KWM1GA5Z09S4482CTVFG9BHC.title: Nectar Pruning CLI
01KWM1GA5Z09S4482CTVFG9BHC.description: Implements the `nectar prune` command logic for deleting expired nectars. It identifies candidates whose paths are no longer on disk and whose last update falls outside a grace period, offering both preview and confirmed deletion modes.

01KWM1GA61N9DSPAQ9ZV876ACM.title: src/registration/review-cli.ts
01KWM1GA61N9DSPAQ9ZV876ACM.description: This module implements the logic for the `nectar review-matches` CLI command, enabling operators to accept, reject, or skip low-confidence code migration candidates. It handles the decision-making process for pending reviews, ensuring data consistency and preventing stale updates.

01KWM1GA63RWPM037TQKGK5NAM.title: src/registration/review-store.ts
01KWM1GA63RWPM037TQKGK5NAM.description: This module defines the interface and implementations for storing pending review candidates. It supports in-memory and file-backed storage, ensuring that `review-matches` CLI can access candidates queued by the daemon. Critical sections are protected by a file-based advisory lock to prevent race conditions during read-modify-write operations.

01KWM1GA65D36Z48B7RX3ENT65.title: File Registration Service
01KWM1GA65D36Z48B7RX3ENT65.description: Orchestrates the end-to-end file registration pipeline, from filesystem observations to processing through the re-association ladder and persisting to the HiveGraphStore. It handles debouncing, resyncs, path classification, and state repair.

01KWM1GA68YRC1WRGV7WTEG5GW.title: Synchronous/Asynchronous Store Bridge
01KWM1GA68YRC1WRGV7WTEG5GW.description: Provides a synchronous `HiveGraphStore` interface over an asynchronous durable store, using an in-memory mirror. It ensures ordered writes, hydration from the durable store, and robust error surfacing for failed durable flushes.

01KWM1GA6AGE2XX896T32PVKE0.title: src/registration/tlsh.ts
01KWM1GA6AGE2XX896T32PVKE0.description: This module provides a pure TypeScript implementation of a locality-sensitive fuzzy fingerprinting algorithm (similar to TLSH) for comparing code content. It computes fingerprints, calculates distances, and maps them to confidence scores, with safeguards for small files and tunable confidence thresholds. This is a default implementation intended for eventual replacement by a native or WASM version.

01KWM1GA6CK1X5XZZK0JV6DHME.title: Nectar HTTP Server
01KWM1GA6CK1X5XZZK0JV6DHME.description: This file defines the HTTP server for Nectar, built using Node.js's native `node:http` module to ensure zero runtime dependencies. It handles `/health` endpoints, dispatches API requests through a `NectarRouter`, and manages server lifecycle including graceful shutdown. The server supports a configurable host and port.

01KWM1GA6EN9J3P77YQ95CD3Q8.title: Service Argv Construction
01KWM1GA6EN9J3P77YQ95CD3Q8.description: This module defines the exact command-line arguments (argv) for installing, uninstalling, and querying the nectar OS service across different platforms (launchd, systemd, schtasks, sc.exe). It serves as the single source of truth for these commands, ensuring consistency and testability.

01KWM1GA6GTGANW28936Q7EDTC.title: Command Runner Interface
01KWM1GA6GTGANW28936Q7EDTC.description: This module provides an injectable interface for running external commands, crucial for testing the service management logic. It uses `node:child_process.execFile` to prevent shell injection and maps all command outcomes (success, failure, timeout) to a `CommandResult` object, never throwing errors.

01KWM1GA6JZ0ZNW1HBZD696PHV.title: Nectar OS Service Manager
01KWM1GA6JZ0ZNW1HBZD696PHV.description: This is the core module for managing the nectar OS service, handling installation, uninstallation, and status checks. It orchestrates the platform-specific argv generation, unit file writing, and command execution, while ensuring crash-safety and graceful degradation for file system or command failures.

01KWM1GA6MM6FAJ695HJAZ9A2J.title: Service Platform Resolution
01KWM1GA6MM6FAJ695HJAZ9A2J.description: This module determines the appropriate service manager and scope (user or system) for nectar based on the operating system, user privileges, and configuration. It normalizes platform names and calculates unit file paths, ensuring consistent service registration across macOS, Linux, and Windows.

01KWM1GA6PH6Z5EWXMYX3H6T4W.title: OS Service Unit Templates
01KWM1GA6PH6Z5EWXMYX3H6T4W.description: This module contains the templated definitions for OS service units (plist, systemd unit, scheduled task XML) for nectar. It ensures that the generated unit files enable essential self-supervision features like restart-on-crash and start-on-boot across different platforms.

01KWM1GA6REK2THZ0MZJN5WPWH.title: Telemetry Check-in Writer
01KWM1GA6REK2THZ0MZJN5WPWH.description: This module manages the runtime check-in process for nectar's telemetry, writing `service_status` records to a local SQLite database. It distinguishes between initial check-ins (new binding time) and periodic heartbeats (updating last-seen time), providing crucial information for health monitoring by doctor.

01KWM1GA6TJD71BKC9JBJGJ0T6.title: Telemetry Database Plumbing
01KWM1GA6TJD71BKC9JBJGJ0T6.description: This module provides the core `node:sqlite` plumbing for nectar's local telemetry database. It defines the schema for `service_status`, `service_metrics`, and `service_logs` tables, handles database opening in WAL mode, and ensures fail-soft behavior if SQLite or file system operations fail.

01KWM1GA6WFHKSN7YWP2F7FXDC.title: Fail-soft Telemetry Facade
01KWM1GA6WFHKSN7YWP2F7FXDC.description: This module offers a fail-soft facade for nectar's telemetry system, integrating check-in, metrics, and log writers. It ensures that any failure in opening or interacting with the local SQLite database gracefully degrades to a no-op, preventing disruptions to the daemon's boot or pipeline.

01KWM1GA6ZD7N2B89A53F5ZER6.title: Logs Telemetry Writer
01KWM1GA6ZD7N2B89A53F5ZER6.description: This file implements a bounded, rotated log emission system using SQLite for local storage. It captures existing structured daemon logs, redacts sensitive information, and enforces retention policies based on age rather than row count.

01KWM1GA72QG53NZSZK0F8XAT9.title: Metrics Telemetry Writer
01KWM1GA72QG53NZSZK0F8XAT9.description: This module provides a metrics snapshot writer for capturing pure counts of pipeline events since the current process's start. It integrates with the HiveGraphStore to automatically increment counters for nectar minting, hive graph versions, descriptions generated, and embeddings computed, ensuring metrics are reset on restart and recorded durably.

01KWM1GA74YP5N8QHJ2ZM56GF6.title: Usage Telemetry Emitter
01KWM1GA74YP5N8QHJ2ZM56GF6.description: This file is the single chokepoint for sending anonymous usage telemetry events (installed, uninstalled, first_run, updated) to PostHog. It implements strict gating, opt-out mechanisms, deduplication, and fail-soft behavior to ensure privacy, reliability, and minimal impact on application performance.

01KWM1GA77RV1Y3Q66T8Y8VFVS.title: PostHog Key Configuration
01KWM1GA77RV1Y3Q66T8Y8VFVS.description: This file defines placeholder constants for the PostHog API key and host for usage telemetry. These values are empty by default and are intended to be replaced during the release build process, ensuring that development and source builds do not send telemetry.

01KWM1GA79H1FVM8GKQBXPDGN3.title: Hiveantennae Worker Harness
01KWM1GA79H1FVM8GKQBXPDGN3.description: This module implements the core worker harness for hiveantennae, based on a lease-driven model for processing various job kinds like brooding, reassociation, and projection sync. It provides a flexible, testable engine with methods for running jobs once, starting/stopping continuous polls, and handling job failures.

01KWM1GA7C6KS8652079ZS9SRG.title: API Router Tests
01KWM1GA7C6KS8652079ZS9SRG.description: This file contains comprehensive tests for the nectar API router, covering its route-group scaffolding, permission middleware inheritance, 501 scaffold for unimplemented paths, and the `mountHiveGraphApi`'s safe no-op behavior. It also includes integration tests for real socket communication, demonstrating how API endpoints are gated and how malformed JSON bodies are handled.

01KWM1GA7FAVT7SG1NP3F22K8M.title: test/bake-posthog-key.test.ts
01KWM1GA7FAVT7SG1NP3F22K8M.description: Tests the `bake-posthog-key.mjs` script, which rewrites the PostHog key at release-time. Verifies that keyed bakes emit loadable ESM with baked values, unkeyed runs are no-ops, and missing target files cause failures.

01KWM1GA7HZHCW449TZ6VFQ6XR.title: test/bridge-ac.test.ts
01KWM1GA7HZHCW449TZ6VFQ6XR.description: Tests the async/sync brood bridge functionality, covering the async-native brood pipeline, the live API endpoint for hive graph builds, the daemon's auto-brood path, and the enricher store's hydration from the async store. The suite uses in-memory fakes for offline testing.

01KWM1GA7M519B65ETNSMPHCK8.title: Brooding Pipeline Tests
01KWM1GA7M519B65ETNSMPHCK8.description: Comprehensive tests for the brooding pipeline (PRD-007), verifying file discovery, content hashing, bucketing, cost estimation, and resume capabilities. Includes tests for prompt injection hardening and dry run functionality.

01KWM1GA7PS9GAY6GWKTYC4Z0X.title: CLI Verb Tests (brood, prune, review-matches)
01KWM1GA7PS9GAY6GWKTYC4Z0X.description: Tests for the CLI verbs `brood`, `prune`, and `review-matches`, ensuring they interact correctly with the durable store. Verifies preview functionality, acceptance of candidates, and interactive decision making for pending reviews.

01KWM1GA7SAHV5SAYG84B940CP.title: CodeRabbit Remediation Tests (CR-1, CR-5, CR-18 findings)
01KWM1GA7SAHV5SAYG84B940CP.description: This file contains tests addressing specific findings from CodeRabbit PRs, such as disk-fs behavior, ignore configurations, prune logic, review acceptance, and FilePendingReviewStore locking mechanisms. It ensures various remediation actions and edge cases are handled correctly.

01KWM1GA7V0JG4ZVST2054FTGV.title: Configuration Validation Tests (EX-2)
01KWM1GA7V0JG4ZVST2054FTGV.description: This suite extensively tests the `config.ts` module, specifically focusing on `envInt` parsing behavior for environment variables like `NECTAR_PORT` and `NECTAR_POLL_INTERVAL_MS`. It asserts that invalid or out-of-range values lead to clear `ConfigError` instances, preventing silent truncation or unsafe configurations, and that defaults are respected.

01KWM1GA7XDB6GFDNW1EZXGQ59.title: Daemon Watcher Integration Test (PRD-018b, AC-018b.2/9)
01KWM1GA7XDB6GFDNW1EZXGQ59.description: This end-to-end integration test verifies the daemon's `fs.watch` functionality. It simulates file system events (create, edit) within a temporary workspace and asserts that the daemon correctly processes these changes, updates the durable store, and handles shutdown cleanly, preventing post-shutdown modifications from being processed.

01KWM1GA802TYZP389Z6RAVPY7.title: Daemon Lifecycle and Health Tests (AC-018a, AC-018b, PRD-017a, PRD-018k)
01KWM1GA802TYZP389Z6RAVPY7.description: This file tests various aspects of the Nectar daemon's lifecycle, including binding to ports, health endpoints, lock management, shutdown procedures, and telemetry integration. It also covers daemon startup prerequisites and their impact on brooding observability.

01KWM1GA83K8VK70Z0GWW3YMDA.title: Disk File System Utility Tests (PRD-018c, NEC-007, NEC-034, NEC-042)
01KWM1GA83K8VK70Z0GWW3YMDA.description: These tests validate the behavior of `createDiskRegistrationFs` and `probeCaseInsensitiveFs` functions. They ensure correct pruning of ignored directories, proper handling of symlinked files during directory walks and existence checks, and the robustness of the filesystem case-insensitivity probe under various conditions.

01KWM1GA85XRDNFG67SXGPHG2K.title: Documentation Linter Tests (PRD-018l, NEC-004, NEC-040, PRD-018k)
01KWM1GA85XRDNFG67SXGPHG2K.description: This suite lints the Nectar documentation to ensure consistency, accuracy, and adherence to updated terminology and CLI commands. It checks for deprecated phrases, verifies the existence of documented CLI commands, and confirms consistent cost figures and prerequisites for daemon functionalities.

01KWM1GA87RJ8H4D8MYMSPJNPB.title: Doctor Registry Integration Tests (AC-018a.9, AC-017a.1.1)
01KWM1GA87RJ8H4D8MYMSPJNPB.description: This test file verifies the functionality of `doctor-registry.ts`, which manages daemon registration with a central Doctor service. It ensures correct registry entry creation, updates, and persistence, specifically focusing on telemetry paths, restart policies, and atomic file operations for robustness.

01KWM1GA8934557A01DAP6G46E.title: Embeddings Dimension Guard Tests (PRD-014, AC-3, US-014b.2, US-014c.3)
01KWM1GA8934557A01DAP6G46E.description: This test suite validates the embedding dimension guard, ensuring that vectors not precisely 768-dimensions are consistently discarded to null. It covers both local and hosted providers, and confirms that rejections are reported through an injectable sink, maintaining data integrity and system robustness.

01KWM1GA8B7V7MJJS4GA7M4S7Y.title: Hosted Embeddings Transport Tests via Portkey (PRD-014b)
01KWM1GA8B7V7MJJS4GA7M4S7Y.description: This file tests the hosted embeddings transport, particularly its integration with Portkey. It verifies correct API endpoint usage, header construction (including rerank-pattern headers), model configuration, and output dimension handling. It also confirms fail-soft behavior, retry mechanisms for rate limits, and secure handling of API keys.

01KWM1GA8DG6VFRW54HK71YHKW.title: Embeddings Provider Selection and Failure Tests (PRD-014a, PRD-014c)
01KWM1GA8DG6VFRW54HK71YHKW.description: This suite validates the embeddings provider selection logic in Nectar. It tests default provider resolution (to local), explicit provider selection (local/hosted/off), and various failure scenarios (dead local daemon, network errors, throwing transports). It ensures fail-soft behavior, where errors result in null embeddings rather than system crashes.

01KWM1GA8FK5N1SVW84RPMB2J2.title: Enricher Steady-State Loop Tests
01KWM1GA8FK5N1SVW84RPMB2J2.description: This file contains comprehensive unit tests for the enricher's steady-state operation, covering various scenarios like content changes, batch processing, error handling, and projection triggers. It validates the enricher's core functionalities, including cosmetic inheritance, meaningful change detection, and LLM interaction.

01KWM1GA8HA5PMX50PADBMA4YH.title: test/fixes.test.ts
01KWM1GA8HA5PMX50PADBMA4YH.description: This file contains regression tests for various fixes addressing CodeRabbit findings and PRD-018k/l requirements. It covers blank environment variable normalization, worker job completion handling, poll loop generation guarding, concurrent daemon startup, and configuration file parsing.

01KWM1GA8J7SV8PCKJ6F17M4E4.title: test/health.test.ts
01KWM1GA8J7SV8PCKJ6F17M4E4.description: Tests the `HealthState` class, verifying that a fresh instance reports 'ok' status and correct initial values for various subsystems. It also covers the logic for degrading the health status, reporting brooding states, and mapping health statuses to HTTP codes.

01KWM1GA8NBTG12AYCGF7NA72W.title: test/hive-graph-api.test.ts
01KWM1GA8NBTG12AYCGF7NA72W.description: This file tests the Hive Graph API endpoints, including search, build, status, and projection, as defined in PRD-008b, 008c, and 012b. It uses a router to exercise handlers with injected dependencies, simulating interactions without a live backend.

01KWM1GA8QVVNEA3V6W5VCZCTP.title: test/hive-graph-deeplake.test.ts
01KWM1GA8QVVNEA3V6W5VCZCTP.description: This file contains unit tests for the Deep Lake integration components, including SQL query guards, schema definitions, and credentials handling. It also extensively tests the `DeepLakeHiveGraphStore` against a mocked transport layer to verify data storage and retrieval logic for identities and versions.

01KWM1GA8SF0B12QSEGAQQ8XCT.title: test/hive-graph-search-live.test.ts
01KWM1GA8SF0B12QSEGAQQ8XCT.description: This file runs a live probe to verify Deep Lake vector ordering for search results (PRD-018h-AC-1). It uses real Deep Lake credentials to insert specific data with controlled embedding vectors and then asserts the search returns them in the expected order, indicating correct semantic matching.

01KWM1GA8T72FE8B1EAF7CWCBT.title: Hive Graph Search Test Suite
01KWM1GA8T72FE8B1EAF7CWCBT.description: Tests for the Hive Graph search functionality, covering lexical and semantic search arms, ranking, degradation, and error handling. It validates SQL generation, search result fusion, and the system's resilience to various failure modes and data inconsistencies.

01KWM1GA8WB926BZS4TMGZB87E.title: Hive Graph Core Functionality Test Suite
01KWM1GA8WB926BZS4TMGZB87E.description: Comprehensive tests for core Hive Graph utilities including ULID generation and parsing, SHA-256 hashing, path manipulation, and schema validation. It also includes an in-memory store implementation to test identity and version management within the graph.

01KWM1GA8YA16H65QDZ2Y6BP84.title: Registration: Ignore Tests
01KWM1GA8YA16H65QDZ2Y6BP84.description: Tests for the ignore rules and predicates used in the registration process. This includes default ignores, graph-ignore file parsing, and integration with Git's `ls-files` and `check-ignore`.

01KWM1GA90024P7WMWSXM2N4YP.title: Single Instance Lock Tests
01KWM1GA90024P7WMWSXM2N4YP.description: Tests for the single instance lock mechanism, ensuring daemon uniqueness and proper handling of lock file creation, reclamation of stale locks, and atomic release.

01KWM1GA92EK0Y6Z0NE3S9Y2KX.title: PollLoop Test Suite
01KWM1GA92EK0Y6Z0NE3S9Y2KX.description: This file contains a comprehensive test suite for the `PollLoop` class. It verifies various behaviors including idle state handling, debouncing, backoff mechanisms, and error handling, utilizing a `manualTimer` for deterministic testing.

01KWM1GA93KYHASDHA63RF9B3T.title: Portkey Gateway Integration Tests (PRD-010)
01KWM1GA93KYHASDHA63RF9B3T.description: Tests the integration with the Portkey API Gateway for AI model interactions, covering header construction, model resolution, retry mechanisms, and error handling. Asserts correct URL and header usage, model ID propagation, and robust transport behavior.

01KWM1GA95NM90KWBXA5YP9CF2.title: Project Scope Resolution Tests (ADR-0002)
01KWM1GA95NM90KWBXA5YP9CF2.description: Verifies the project scope resolution logic, which determines the active project ID based on a cascade of sources: environment variables, file system bindings, Git remote signals, and a fallback to an 'unsorted' inbox. Includes tests for cache handling, malformed data, and tenancy guards.

01KWM1GA98F18SESBSFJFJ51QS.title: Projection Module Acceptance Tests (PRD-018i, NEC-019)
01KWM1GA98F18SESBSFJFJ51QS.description: Covers critical acceptance criteria for the Projection module, including atomic file writing, debounced updates, rebuilds from a store, and handling of projection schema versions and tenancy mismatches. Verifies content hash handling, inheritance logic, and stability of serialization.

01KWM1GA9A1QC9657AJPJRND9S.title: Pruning CLI Tests (AC-19)
01KWM1GA9A1QC9657AJPJRND9S.description: Tests the `prune` command-line interface for the Hive Graph, ensuring it correctly identifies and deletes nectars based on their age and disk presence. Verifies preview mode, forced deletion, and grace period logic.

01KWM1GA9CCKC5NVW9VPKGG9WZ.title: Registration Service Core Logic Tests (AC-4, AC-9, AC-3, AC-5, PRD-018c NEC-008,
01KWM1GA9CCKC5NVW9VPKGG9WZ.description: Comprehensive tests for the Registration Service, covering burst handling, isolated failure, rename reconstruction, full resync on null observation, and ignored paths. Includes testing for fuzzy matching, pending review queues, cold catch-up on boot, watcher restart logic, and case-insensitive renames.

01KWM1GA9E9FMXVXJYDTMF5F1Q.title: Registration Ladder and Watch Intake Tests (AC-018l.16, AC-018c.8, AC-018c.9, AC
01KWM1GA9E9FMXVXJYDTMF5F1Q.description: Tests the core 'ladder' logic for file change classification and reassociation within the Hive Graph system. Covers debounced watch events, path classification, the five steps of the reassociation ladder (no-op, append, carry, match, mint), stat caching, case-insensitive renames, and crash recovery scenarios for identity and version rows.

01KWM1GA9G2PSZ6V0AQVZA7RMQ.title: Review Matches CLI Tests (AC-18, AC-018d.5, AC-018d.6, AC-018d.7, AC-018d.8)
01KWM1GA9G2PSZ6V0AQVZA7RMQ.description: Tests the `review-matches` CLI for processing pending fuzzy matches, including accepting, rejecting, and skipping candidates. Verifies concurrent operations on the review store, handling of stale candidates, and crash recovery during acceptance.

01KWM1GA9H9HQRFZT6XEEKDPB0.title: Search CLI Loopback Tests (PRD-012b)
01KWM1GA9H9HQRFZT6XEEKDPB0.description: Tests the `nectar search` command-line interface as a thin client for the daemon's search API. Verifies argument parsing, daemon communication, error handling for unreachable daemons, and ensures byte-identical JSON output between the CLI and raw API endpoint. Also checks human-readable rendering.

01KWM1GA9KGEQKY1HHHQ204P5M.title: Security Remediation Regression Tests - Wave B
01KWM1GA9KGEQKY1HHHQ204P5M.description: Regression tests for security fixes related to Portkey, projection, and embeddings modules.
